title: MOZ and GMB Specialist
description:
We’re looking for a MOZ & Google My Business (GMB) Specialist to support our SEO efforts and ensure our clients’ business listings are accurate, optimized, and live across key platforms. Your work will directly support local visibility and help our clients get found more easily online.  



Key Responsibilities:



Manage and optimize Google My Business (GMB) profiles

Evaluate client eligibility and submit business listings via Moz Local following SOP guidelines.

Monitor Moz Local submissions and ensure listings go live successfully.

Fix listing inconsistencies, incomplete data, and citation issues where possible.

Coordinate internally on unresolved Moz or GMB issues with clear details.

Send client updates after successful Moz submissions using provided templates.

Handle IdeaMensch submissions as a secondary task when needed.



Qualifications Required:



Hands-on experience with Moz Local (must-have).

Experience managing Google My Business (GMB) profiles.

Solid understanding of local SEO, NAP consistency, and business listings.

Strong attention to detail and ability to follow SOPs.

Clear written English communication.

Able to work independently and meet deadlines.



Nice to Have:



Familiarity with LocalFalcon

Experience with IdeaMensch submissions.

Background working with SEO agencies

Familiarity with citation management tools other than Moz.

Basic SEO reporting or dashboard experience.



We don’t micromanage. Just bring your A-game, stay committed, and get things done.

If this sounds like the right fit for you, please fill out our application form:

title: Virtual Assistant (Social Media & Operations)
description:
About the Engagement 



We are seeking a highly organized, detail?oriented Virtual Assistant (VA) to provide administrative support and take ownership of our social media presence. This is a contractor engagement, not an employment role. The successful contractor will operate as an independent service provider, managing their tools, and work methods while delivering agreed?upon outcomes. 



 We prefer contractors with experience supporting international clients and who are comfortable working independently with minimal supervision. 



Scope of Work (Deliverables?Based) 



Social Media Management & Content Creation 



• Independently manage and operate assigned social media accounts (e.g.,
, Instagram, LinkedIn) 



• Create original social media content, including captions, basic graphics, and short?form posts 



• Plan, draft, and schedule posts aligned with brand voice and objectives 



• Monitor engagement and provide simple performance summaries 



• Take ownership of content quality, consistency, and timely delivery 



 



Administrative & Operational Support 



• Complete defined administrative tasks such as inbox support, answering office calls, communications, data entry, document preparation, and basic research, 



• Assist with day?to?day operational tasks based on clearly defined deliverables 



• Proactively identify improvements and efficiencies within assigned work 



Required Qualifications 



• Prior experience as a Virtual Assistant or similar freelance role 



• Strong written and verbal English skills with a clear or neutral accent 



• Proven ability to work independently and deliver quality results with minimal supervision 



• Reliable internet connection and backup power 



• Ability to take calls or respond during agreed?upon availability windows 



 



Preferred Qualifications 



• Experience managing social media accounts for businesses 



• Familiarity with remote collaboration tools and office software 



• Strong time?management and organizational skills 



• Experience working with international clients 



 



Independent Contractor Requirements 



• Operate as a freelancer or independent business 



• Use own equipment, software, and work environment 



• Control where work is completed 



• Responsible for own taxes and statutory obligations 





No employment benefits are provided under this contract. 



 Engagement & Compensation 



• Contract for services with defined scope and deliverables 



• Initial engagement with renewal option 



• Starting rate: $5 CAD per hour (alternative pricing may be discussed) 



• Starting half time, should be available 9 AM to 1 PM PST, with possibility to convert to full time. 





 



How to Apply 



Please submit: 



• A brief introduction outlining your VA experience 



• Examples of social media work 



• Your proposed rate structure

title: S-kin Studio - All-Rounder Marketing & Customer Service VA
description:
Job Title: All-Rounder Marketing & Customer Service VA

Job Type: Full-time | Remote

Hours: 8AM–5PM AEDT (Melbourne, Australia time zone)

Contract: Minimum 18 months | 3-month probation

Salary: Competitive (based on experience), we'd rather find the right person than the cheapest one



About Us

S-kin Studio is a Melbourne-based jewellery ecommerce brand with a cult following and a very specific kind of magic. We make culturally-inspired, limited-edition pieces, such as Lunar New Year capsule drops, anime-inspired collections, Tamagotchi lockets, and collectible charm systems that make people say "omg where did you get that." We're women-led, founder-driven, and very online.

We're a small but mighty team of gen-z girlies (led by a millennial CEO who keeps everyone grounded) and we work fast, care deeply, and genuinely love what we make. If you've ever saved a jewellery post at midnight or sent a "this is SO you" link to a friend, you might be our person.

We're growing, we're scaling into the US market, and we need someone who wants to grow with us.



About the Role

We're looking for a bubbly, sharp, and genuinely passionate All-Rounder VA to sit across two critical areas of the business: paid ads & marketing execution, and customer service.

This isn't a role for someone who wants to show up, tick boxes, and clock off. You'll be moving fast, handling real volume, working with a real brand, and expected to actually think. The upside? You'll be working on one of the most exciting jewellery brands in Australia, with a team that actually has fun doing it.

Think of it as the perfect mix of Dwight Schrute's systems brain and Elle Woods' warmth and energy - or as we like to say, a genuine unicorn. 

You will report directly to and work closely with our founder, marketing team, and operations team.



What You'll Be Doing

Meta Ads & Marketing (your biggest focus - up to 20 hrs/week, more during campaigns):

- Upload and set up 10–40 Meta ad creatives per week in Ads Manager (yes, it gets busy during sales but that's the fun part)

- Write punchy, on-brand ad copy for each creative using our AI-assisted copywriting system

- Structure campaigns and ad sets according to our internal $100
ta Ad Framework

- Keep our creative asset library clean, named correctly, and easy to navigate

- Run daily QA checks on all live ads & catch errors before they cost us

- Pull weekly performance reports for our marketing lead



Customer Service (the heart of the brand - 12–15 hrs/week, more during sales):

- Own all customer conversations via Richpanel across
, Instagram DMs, and

- Handle order issues with warmth and speed - missing parcels, wrong items, returns, exchanges, refunds

- Process returns via Redo and create shipping labels via DHL and Australia Post

- Manage workshop and piercing appointment enquiries

- Be the kind of CS rep that turns a frustrated customer into a loyal one



Influencer &
 Support:

- Keep our influencer gifting pipeline running end-to-end - briefs, follow-ups, tracking, reporting

- Build and schedule
 campaigns in Klaviyo from briefs provided by the marketing team



Operations & Admin (light but important):

- Flag low stock before it becomes a problem

- Keep SOPs, trackers, and Shopify up to date

- Be the person who notices things and says something before being asked





About You

- You have at least 2 years of experience in an ecommerce or digital marketing VA role - ideally with a DTC or fashion/lifestyle brand

- You're fluent in Meta Ads Manager and can set up a campaign confidently

- Your written English is excellent - you write like a human, (not a robot), and you know how to match a brand's tone and humanize AI copy

- You're a natural in customer-facing situations - empathetic, quick, and never stiff or robotic

- You are obsessively organised - trackers make you happy, things slipping through the cracks make you anxious (in a good way)

- You thrive independently but communicate proactively - you don't disappear, you don't wait to be chased

- You can handle high-volume periods without losing quality - sale weeks don't scare you, they excite you

- You actually love jewellery, fashion, or lifestyle brands - this isn't just a job to you, it's a world you want to be in

Shopify, Meta, Klaviyo experience is a big green flag

- Experience with Australian brands or clients is a bonus





What We Offer

- Competitive salary based on your experience and how you perform in our screening process

- Fully remote role with structured hours (8AM–5PM AEDT) so you always know when you're on and off

- 10 days sick leave + public holiday leave + 2 weeks paid personal leave

- A brand you'll actually be proud to tell people you work for

- Work directly with a founder-led team that's building something real

- Clear onboarding, SOPs, and a team that wants you to succeed 

- Free jewellery budget (1-2 pieces every quarter)

- Real room to grow as we scale in Australia and the US - this is a long-term role for the right person



About the Team

We're a group of passionate, creative, slightly chaotic-in-the-best-way girlies who take the work seriously but not ourselves. Our CEO Chi is a Vietnamese-Australian creative entrepreneur who built this brand from the ground up. Our team is small, close-knit, and genuinely fun to work with.

We're not looking for someone who just needs a job. We're looking for someone who sees this posting and thinks "that's me."

If you're the kind of person who notices when something's off-brand before being told, sends a "have you seen this??" link to the team chat, and feels genuine excitement when a campaign performs - we want to hear from you. ????



How to Apply

PLEASE Read this carefully - we actually mean it. Incomplete applications will not be reviewed.

Step 1: Complete this personality assessment before you apply (it takes ~10 minutes):



Step 2: Send everything below to 
 with the subject line: VA Application – [Your Name]



Your CV / resume

A cover letter (max 1 page) - tell us why this role, one example of a time you handled serious workload pressure, and what you already know about S-kin Studio. We read every single one, so if you don't send one, we'll assume you're bulk applying and we'll wish you well elsewhere ????

A short video introduction (2–3 minutes) - tell us who you are, your experience with Meta Ads and customer service, and why you'd be brilliant for this role.

Your Testgorilla results - screenshot of your full results page + 2–3 sentences on whether you think it's accurate

Your portfolio or any work samples you're proud of - ad copy, CS responses, campaign results, anything that shows us what you can do



Applications close: 31 March 2026

We review every application personally and shortlisted candidates will be invited to complete a short skills screening task before being invited to interview.

title: AI/ML Engineer (Contract – Remote)
description:
** Salary: Php 70,000 - 80,000 / month

** Location: Remote

** Contract: 3 Months

** Experience: 4–5 years



We are looking for a skilled AI/ML Engineer to help build intelligent systems that optimize infrastructure performance, cost efficiency, and resource allocation. This role focuses on designing and deploying machine learning models that support automated decision-making across cloud and containerized environments.



You will work closely with engineering and DevOps teams to develop scalable AI-driven solutions that process infrastructure metrics and generate real-time optimization strategies for modern systems and workloads



Key Responsibilities



- Design and deploy machine learning models for infrastructure optimization

- Develop algorithms for predictive modeling and decision optimization

- Build data pipelines and feature engineering workflows

- Integrate ML outputs into backend services through APIs

- Monitor and retrain models using modern MLOps practices

- Implement intelligent scheduling and resource allocation systems



Required Skills



- 4–5 years experience building production ML systems

- Strong Python development skills

- Experience with PyTorch, TensorFlow, scikit-learn

- Knowledge of Kubernetes and containerized environments

- Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP)

- Familiarity with Docker, Cassandra/PostgreSQL, Redis, Kafka

- Experience with ML lifecycle tools (MLflow, Kubeflow, Airflow)



Nice to Have



- Experience with recommendation systems or optimization engines

- Knowledge of GPU infrastructure and CUDA environments

- This is a great opportunity to contribute to innovative AI systems and work with modern infrastructure technologies.



--> Please submit your CV and expected availability when applying.

title: Client Call Coordinator
description:
We are a fast-growing structural engineering firm based in Australia and is currently looking for a confident and composed Client Call Coordinator with a strong direct background in client calls and client meetings and has worked in a coordinator project management before. A full-time role from 7:00 AM to 3:30 pm Philippines time, weekdays only.



This role involves direct communication with clients— particularly those related to project delays, drawing status, or technical concerns. Your key responsibility is to act as the first point of contact on the phone, provide a warm and professional experience for the client, and coordinate internally with our design team to ensure timely resolutions.



We are ideally looking for someone who is comfortable under pressure, has excellent communication skills, and enjoys solving  in a fast-paced environment.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES:



Answer incoming client phone calls regarding project updates, delays, or technical questions.



Reassure and guide clients calmly and professionally — especially in high-pressure or escalated situations.



Gather relevant information from clients to assist internal design teams.



Follow up on requests with the design team and ensure clients receive timely responses.



Maintain detailed records of calls and follow-ups using internal systems.



Escalate urgent or complex issues to the appropriate internal tea
mbers.



Build and maintain strong client relationships through consistent and reliable communication.



Assist in improving call-handling procedures and response workflows.



SKILLS & QUALIFICATIONS:



A university degree or diploma



Previous experience in a client-facing or coordination role is highly desirable.



Excellent verbal communication skills with a calm and empathetic phone manner.



Ability to handle pressure, de-escalate tense situations, and remain composed under stress.



Strong organizational and time management skills.



Comfortable with regular phone interaction as the core part of the role.



Basic understanding of engineering or construction drawings is a PLUS.



Proficient in using collaboration tools (e.g., Teams, CRM systems, call logging software).



NICE TO HAVE:



Familiarity with the design and construction industry.



Experience working with internal engineering or design teams.



Prior work in a support or help desk-style role involving technical discussions.



To apply and be considered, please follow the below: 



1. Please send a link to your most updated CV and a short recording link explaining your relevant experience to the role.

2. Use 3 apply points.



Only those applicants who follow the above instructions will be contacted.

title: One-on-One English Education Institution - Filipino Tutor Resource Partner Recruitment
description:
Requirements for Partner Agencies/Individuals

1. Direct Resource Access: Must have direct access to a pool of Filipino tutors(no multi-layer intermediaries) to ensure stable supply and competitive pricing.

2. Pre-Screening Capability: Ability to conduct initial screenings (document verification, oral English assessment, attitude evaluation) before submitting candidates.

3. Attitude & Quality Control: Strictly exclude tutors with poor work attitude, lack of professionalism, or weak communication skills.

4. Operational Support: Assist in coordinating the interview schedules, facilitating onboarding, and handling basic communication with tutors during their employment (e.g., schedule adjustments).

5. Integrity: Ensure all submitted tutor information and certificates are 100% authentic. Any fraudulent information will result in immediate termination of cooperation.

6. SERIOUS INQUIRIES ONLY: We are seeking long-term partners, not one-time suppliers.

 

Our Cooperation Advantages

1. Stable & Large Demand: We have a large base of young students with dense weekly class schedules, ensuring a steady flow of tutor placements for our partners.

2. Efficient Process: A dedicated account manager will handle communications. Our verification process is transparent and efficient, minimize waiting time.

3. Guaranteed Payments: Flexible and reliable payment terms (Monthly or Weekly settlements available). We maintain a zero-tolerance policy for delayed payments.

4. Exclusive Partnership: High-performing partners (providing high-quality tutors and excellent service) will be offered exclusive cooperation rights for all our future tutor needs.

 

Scope of Cooperation Services

1. Candidate Sourcing: Partners source and pre-screen tutors based on our specific requirements (quantity, experience with kids, availability).

2. Interview Coordination: Partners submit shortlisted CVs and assist in scheduling demo lessons between tutors and our academic team.



Requirements for Partner Agencies/Individuals

1. Direct Resource Access: Must have direct access to a pool of Filipino tutors (no multi-layer intermediaries) to ensure stable supply and competitive pricing.

2. Pre-Screening Capability: Ability to conduct initial screenings (document verification, oral English assessment, attitude evaluation) before submitting candidates.

3. Attitude & Quality Control: Strictly exclude tutors with poor work attitude, lack of professionalism, or weak communication skills.

4. Operational Support: Assist in coordinating the interview schedules, facilitating onboarding, and handling basic communication with tutors during their employment (e.g., schedule adjustments).

5. Integrity: Ensure all submitted tutor information and certificates are 100% authentic. Any fraudulent information will result in immediate termination of cooperation.

6. SERIOUS INQUIRIES ONLY: We are seeking long-term partners, not one-time suppliers.

 

Our Cooperation Advantages

1. Stable & Large Demand: We have a large base of young students with dense weekly class schedules, ensuring a steady flow of tutor placements for our partners.

2. Efficient Process: A dedicated account manager will handle communications. Our verification process is transparent and efficient, minimize waiting time.

3. Guaranteed Payments: Flexible and reliable payment terms (Monthly or Weekly settlements available). We maintain a zero-tolerance policy for delayed payments.

4. Exclusive Partnership: High-performing partners (providing high-quality tutors and excellent service) will be offered exclusive cooperation rights for all our future tutor needs.

 

Scope of Cooperation Services

 1. Candidate Sourcing: Partners source and pre-screen tutors based on our specific requirements (quantity, experience with kids, availability).

2. Interview Coordination: Partners submit shortlisted CVs and assist in scheduling demo lessons between tutors and our academic team.

3. Onboarding Support: Assist tutors in completing our internal onboarding process and understanding teaching requirements.

4. Ongoing Communication: Act as a liaison for basic operational issues (e.g., schedule changes, technical difficulties) to ensure smooth teaching operations.



Cooperation Process

1. Initial Contact: Partners contact us with a detailed introduction to their tutor resources (scale, qualifications, supply capacity).

2. Resource Verification: Partners submit a sample of 3-5 qualified tutor CVs for our review and demo lesson assessment.

3. Trial Cooperation: A small-batch trial recruitment will be conducted to evaluate resource quality and communication efficiency.

4. Formal Partnership: Upon satisfactory trial results, a formal cooperation agreement is signed, and large-scale tutor supply begins.

 

Note

 

When applying, please attach a brief introduction to your Filipino tutor resource pool (including the number of available tutors, their average teaching experience, and your supply capacity) to facilitate our efficient review.

 

SERIOUS INQUIRIES ONLY!

title: Assistant Operations Manager - Remote| Singapore Company
description:
ASSISTANT OPERATIONS MANAGER — REMOTE | SINGAPORE COMPANY

Slim Sassy Strong Pte Ltd | Remote Full-Time | php20K–45K+ | Singapore travel opportunities



You’re good at your job. You’re just tired of the commute.

You’ve spent years working onsite — handling clients, managing workflows, keeping things running. You’re competent, you’re professional, and you show up.

But the traffic is costing you 2–3 hours every day. Hours you’d rather spend building something.

We’re offering you a way out of the commute — without stepping back in your career.



Slim Sassy Strong is a women’s wellness education company headquartered in Singapore. We are hiring one Assistant Operations Manager to join our remote operations team, with a structured path to Operations Manager and Senior Operations Manager.



This is a serious career role, not a work-from-home side gig.



|Phase   |Role                     |Compensation    |

|--------|-------------------------|----------------|

|Month 1 |Training                 |php20,000 fixed   |

|Phase 2 |AOM (KPI-based)          |php28,000 starting|

|Phase 3 |Operations Manager       |php35,000–php45,000 |

|Advanced|Senior Operations Manager|php45,000+        |



Plus: selected tea
mbers travel to Singapore for company events and operations.

Advancement is based on performance — not time served.



You will work directly with Singapore-based leadership as an operational extension of the business:

	?	Manage executive calendars and protect leadership time

	?	Own structured
 communication and client follow-ups

	?	Track KPIs, deadlines, and team task execution

	?	Conduct client-facing calls — professionally and confidently

	?	Support the consult pipeline and marketing-to-ops handover

	?	Maintain SOP compliance and operational documentation



As you grow into OM, you lead the team, own systems, and make decisions independently.

Client communication is a core part of this role — not an occasional task.



Why this works for you:

	?	No commute. Work from home, full-time, with a Singapore company on your resume.

	?	Real career track — AOM ? OM ? SOM with compensation that grows with your scope

	?	Singapore travel — top performers join us onsite for events and operational stints

	?	Above-market ceiling — ?45K+ for senior performers

	?	Long-term stability — we grow with people who perform; we don’t churn talent



You’ll do well here if:

You’ve worked onsite in operations, client coordination, admin, hospitality, or a similar field. You’re used to being relied on. You’re not looking for an easy job — you’re looking for a better setup to do your best work in.

Specifically, you:

	?	Are confident handling clients directly — calls, not just chat

	?	Stay composed and professional under pressure

	?	Meet deadlines without being chased

	?	Communicate clearly and directly, especially when escalating

	?	Are fast with digital tools and willing to learn new systems

	?	Want to build a career, not just collect a paycheck

	?	You don’t mind putting in extra hours when it counts. You just want those hours to actually mean something.



Technical requirements

	?	Minimum 50 WPM typing speed

	?	Strong written and spoken English — business standard

	?	Stable internet with a documented backup connection

	?	Own laptop suitable for business use

	?	Familiarity with Google Workspace or similar tools



How to apply:



 with the exact subject line:

Assistant Operations Manager Application

Include all of the following — incomplete applications will not be reviewed:

	1.	One paragraph: what you’ve handled onsite and why you’re ready to bring that to a remote leadership role

	2.	Your current city and power/internet stability details

	3.	Device specs + internet speed screenshot

	4.	Your backup plan during outages

	5.	A 1–2 minute informal audio introduction

	6.	DISC profile screenshot (OnlineJobs)

	7.	Typing test screenshot (min. 50 WPM)

title: Amazon Research & Development Specialist

title: AI Narrative Designer (Entry Level - Remote)
description:
(Fresh Graduates are welcome to apply)



Our client is building AI-powered roleplay simulations to help professionals practice real-world conversations. We’re looking for someone who enjoys experimenting with AI tools and has strong written English to help test and refine these simulations.

In this role, you’ll work closely with our team to review, test, and improve AI-generated conversations, ensuring they feel realistic, logical, and culturally appropriate for professional scenarios.

You don’t need a technical background or coding skills. What matters most is clear thinking, strong writing, and curiosity about AI tools like ChatGPT. Fresh Graduates are welcome to apply



What You’ll Do

- Test AI-generated roleplay conversations and evaluate whether they sound natural and realistic

- Refine prompts and instructions to improve AI responses

- Review dialogue for clarity, tone, and logical flow

- Identify where AI outputs feel unnatural or unrealistic

- Suggest improvements to prompts and scenarios

- Run multiple prompt tests to improve conversation quality

- Collaborate with the team to refine training simulations



What We’re Looking For

- A strong English writer and communicator

- Curious about AI tools like ChatGPT, Claude, Perplexity

- Logical and detail-oriented

- Able to think critically about conversations and realism

- Comfortable experimenting and testing different approaches

- Able to work independently and follow structured instructions



Preferred Backgrounds

- Candidates from these fields often do well in this role:

- Communications

- Marketing

- Psychology

- English / Literature

- Content Writing

- Social Media / Digital Marketing



Nice to Have (But Not Required)

- Experience using AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude, etc.)

- Experience with content writing or editing

- Experience reviewing conversations, scripts, or dialogue

- Interest in emerging AI technologies



What Success Looks Like

- Improving the realism and quality of AI-generated conversations

- Identifying logical or cultural gaps in AI responses

- Helping refine prompts to produce better outputs

- Contributing to the development of high-quality roleplay simulations



Please apply through this link

title: SEO Content Writer
description:
We are looking for a highly proficient full time SEO Content Writer.



Area Ten is a global digital agency that services large corporates to fast-growing upstarts across Australia, Asia and North America. We're an SEO agency that needs copy written for a range of clients covering an extensive range of industries/topics. One of the keys to our success lies in the quality of our staff. We have a strict policy of no junior staff which means you’ll be working with a high calibre of colleagues which presents ample opportunities to learn and develop. For a bit more context please feel free to view this video from the founder Jeremy Tang.

Typically, we require 2 to 3 articles per day.

Strong attention to detail

The ability to follow instructions and specific writing guidelines

title: Operations Manager (Remote) - Hospitality
description:
About Us

We manage a growing portfolio of short-term rental properties across two US markets. We operate furnished units on Airbnb, VRBO, and direct booking channels, supported by a remote VA team, a revenue manager, and a local vendor network. We are building the operational infrastructure to scale from our current portfolio to 50+ units.

Why This Role Exists

Our goal is simple: the only things that should require the owner’s time are landlord relationships and final sign-off on major decisions. Everything else — maintenance, vendors, guest problems, compliance — should either be handled by you directly or brought to the owner as a recommendation, not an open question.

We need someone who can own the operational layer: investigate issues, weigh options, make decisions within their authority, and present clear recommendations when something exceeds their limits. You are not a task executor who asks “what should we do?” You are the person who says “here’s what happened, here are the options, and here’s what I recommend.”

What You Will Own

Maintenance, Vendors & Network Building

 ? Triage all maintenance requests and dispatch vendors across both markets — plumbers, electricians, HVAC, pest control, handymen

 ? Proactively source and vet new vendors through cold outreach, pricing benchmarks, and fit assessments — building ranked primary, backup, and emergency options per market

 ? Negotiate vendor rates, manage renewals, verify invoices, and own vendor relationships day-to-day

 ? Track all open maintenance items daily and own the preventive maintenance calendar without being reminded

 ? Handle insurance claims end-to-end: document damage, file, follow up with adjusters

Guest Escalation & Problem Resolution

 ? Handle escalations the messaging VAs cannot resolve: maintenance disruptions, amenity outages, noise complaints, compensation demands

 ? Coordinate emergency responses — lockouts, water leaks, HVAC failures, pest sightings — and draft guest communications for complex situations

 ? Issue refunds and credits within your authority independently, escalate anything above your limit with a recommendation

VA Team Management

 ? Direct and coordinate the work of two remote VAs handling guest messaging and daily admin

 ? Assign tasks, monitor output quality, provide feedback, and serve as their first point of escalation

 ? Identify workflow gaps and propose improvements — they escalate to you, not the owner

Compliance & Monitoring

 ? Maintain a compliance calendar covering STR permits, business licenses, and insurance renewals

 ? Monitor for city and county regulation changes that affect our properties

 ? Review post-clean photos before guest check-in and coordinate same-day cleaner coverage when needed

Decision Authority

This role comes with real authority. You will have a defined spend limit to dispatch vendors, issue guest compensation, and purchase supplies without checking in first. Within those limits, you act — you don’t ask. When something exceeds your authority, you bring a recommendation, not a question. Authority limits expand as you demonstrate judgment.

What We Are Looking For

Required:

 ? 2+ years in short-term rental operations, property management, or hotel/hospitality operations

 ? Comfortable making decisions and taking action independently — not waiting for direction

 ? Experience sourcing and coordinating vendors or contractors, including cold outreach

 ? Strong written and spoken English — guest messages, vendor calls, negotiation
s are daily tasks

 ? Highly organized with the ability to manage multiple open items across several properties simultaneously

 ? Comfortable with Google Workspace, Slack, and Notion

 ? Available at least 6 hours overlap with US Central Time (8am–5pm CT)

Preferred:

 ? Experience with Airbnb/VRBO host-side platforms

 ? Familiarity with property management tools (Guesty, Hospitable, OwnerRez, or similar)

 ? Experience managing or directing other remote workers or VAs

 ? Experience building a vendor network in a market you weren’t physically present in

Who thrives here:

 ? Your instinct when a problem lands is to research, assess options, and bring a recommendation — not ask what to do

 ? You build vendor relationships before you need them, not after

 ? You communicate proactively — the owner never has to ask “what happened with X?” because you already sent the update

 ? You’re comfortable holding others accountable, not just doing everything yourself

 ? You treat the business like it’s yours

How to Apply

Apply through OnlineJobs.ph. Send us the following — incomplete applications will not be reviewed:

 1. Your OnlineJobs.ph profile link

 2. Your expected monthly rate in USD and your available hours in Central Time

 3. A Loom video (under 5 minutes). No scripts, no slides, no prep needed. Turn on the camera and cover three things: who you are and what you’ve done operationally, a specific time you identified a problem, figured out the options, and brought a recommendation to your manager — what happened and what did you d
, and finally how you approach situations where you don’t have all the information and the owner is unreachable. We’re not grading the answer — we’re listening to how you think.

title: UGC Video Editor
description:
Role: UGC Video Editor

Location: Remote (9AM - 1PM UK Time)



Are you more than “just an editor” or someone who just “cuts clips".? We’re looking for someone who creates ads that stop the scroll, drive clicks, and sell products.



If you:

- Know what makes a viewer STOP in the first 3 seconds

- Can take raw UGC and turn it into multiple performance-driven concepts

- Understand that speed + quality = results

- Want to be rewarded for impact, not just output



Then this is for you.



???? The Role

As our Video Editor, you’ll be creating direct-response ad creatives for fast-growing eCommerce brands. This is not just an editing role, you’ll be part of the creative strategy loop, working with our team to:

- Edit scroll-stopping video ads designed to convert

- Iterate & optimize creatives based on real performance data

- Suggest new angles, hooks & formats proactively - not waiting for someone to handhold you

If you thrive in a fast-paced, results-driven environment, where urgency & ownership are valued, you’ll fit right in.



?? What You'll Do

- Edit direct-response video ads using UGC, AI, broll, voiceovers, reviews, and product demos etc.

- Proactively suggest improvements to existing ads to drive further performance.

- Brief in new creative versions or angles based on data (with support from strategy team)



? What We’re Looking For

- 2+ years editing ads specifically for eCommerce

- Proven portfolio of real ad creatives that have driven results (bonus points for metrics)

- Understands direct-response frameworks (Problem ? Solution, PAS, AIDA, etc.)

- Knows the key creative performance metrics (CTR, Thumb Stop Ratio, ROAS, CPA)

- Fast without sacrificing quality - you can turn around edits quickly and meet deadlines consistently

- Proactive & resourceful - you suggest ideas, solve problems, and own your craft

- Comfortable in Premiere Pro, After Effects, CapCut or similar tools



If you just want someone to “hand you clips and tell you what to do,” this isn’t for you.



Note:

This role will start as a contract position, with the possibility of extension based on performance and team requirements.



???? How to Apply

You'll need to include:

- A link to your portfolio

- Examples of ads you’ve edited (with results if possible)

title: Photo Reviewer for Social Media, Simple Task, Long Term Work
description:
e shoot hundreds of photos during photoshoots.

Most photos are not needed. We only want the best ones.

Your job is simple.

You open the folder.

You look at every photo.

You choose the best ones.

You are helping us turn 500 photos into the best 20 to 40 photos for social media.

What You Will Do

• Look through large folders of photos

• Select the best photos for social media

• Skip blurry, dark, or low quality images

• Skip duplicates or weak poses

• Place the best photos in a separate folder

What Makes a Good Photo

Select photos with:

• Clear focus

• Good lighting

• Strong pose

• Clean background

• Good facial expression

Think like this.

If you saw the photo on Instagram, would you stop and look at it?

If yes, select it.

If no, skip it.

Important

You do not edit photos.

You only select the best ones.

Requirements

• Good eye for photos

• Attention to detail

• Ability to review many photos quickly

• Organized and reliable

This is an entry level role.

If you do good work, we will give you more work in the future.

title: PR/INFLUENCER Outreach Assistant
description:
We are a fast-growing eCommerce beauty brand looking for a motivated and creative individual to join our team and support our influencer marketing efforts. This is a long-term role with strong potential for growth. Our company is still young but expanding quickly, and we’re looking for someone who is excited to grow alongside us and become a key part of the brand’s journey.



Your primary responsibility will be influencer outreach and PR coordination. You’ll be responsible for identifying creators who genuinely align with our brand, reaching out to them to collaborate, and coordinating gifted product in exchange for content (posts, videos, etc.).



Key responsibilities

-Research and identify influencers who align with our brand vision and aesthetic

-Organise and manage PR gifting, ensuring products are sent accurately and on time

-Track influencer outreach, PR sent, content deliverables, and follow-up responses

-Help scale our influencer program by managing outreach to a growing number of creators

-Ensure all influencer activity is well-organised and clearly documented



What we’re looking for

-Strong organisation and attention to detail

-An eye for branding and the ability to identify creators who truly fit our vision

-Excellent communication skills

-Proactive, reliable, and excited about influencer marketing, social media, and the beauty space



This role is best suited for someone who genuinely enjoys social media and understands the content and creator space.

It’s ideal for someone seeking hands-on experience in influencer marketing and a position with real growth opportunities as the brand continues to scale. Prior experience is not essential — we’re far more focused on finding someone with the right attitude, strong initiative, and a willingness to learn.



If you’re organised, creative, social-media savvy, and excited by the idea of growing with a fast-moving brand, this role would be a great fit.

title: Coding & Robotics Education Licensee - Master Trainer
description:
Coding & Robotics Education Licensee - Master Trainer







Remote-Based  |  Occasional Travel Required  |  Part-Time / Contract





About Our Company :



We are the leading Coding and Robotics Education Academy in Singapore. Our passion and mission is in teaching the children of today, the skills of tomorrow, and have fun learning it !



About the Role : 



We are seeking a passionate and experienced Coding & Robotics Education Trainer to support our growing network of licensees. The successful candidate will be responsible for training our licensee partners who have adopted our full coding and robotics curriculum, ensuring they are well-equipped to deliver high-quality STEM education programs.



This role is primarily remote-based, with occasional travel required for training sessions, workshops, or partner support.



You will empower our licensees with the knowledge, skills, and confidence to deliver our coding and robotics curriculum effectively to children aged 5 to 14 years old. You will also play a key role in keeping our curriculum fresh, relevant, and engaging by updating existing materials and developing new content as our programmes evolve.



Key Responsibilities :



•	Train and onboard new licensees on our full coding and robotics curriculum, ensuring they are equipped to deliver high-quality lessons.

•	Conduct training workshops and sessions for licensee educators, both virtually and in-person as required.

•	Provide ongoing coaching, mentoring, and support to licensees post-training.

•	Evaluate licensee competency and readiness to deliver the curriculum independently.

•	Review, update, and improve existing curriculum materials to ensure they remain current and effective.

•	Develop new curriculum modules and lesson plans aligned with industry trends and educational best practices.

•	Collaborate with the curriculum and product teams to align training delivery with programme goals.

•	Gather feedback from licensees and learners to continuously improve training programmes.

•	Maintain comprehensive training documentation, guides, and resources.

•	Travel occasionally to licensee locations for in-person training, support, and quality assurance.



Who We Are Looking For :



We are seeking someone who combines a love for education technology with real-world teaching experience. The ideal candidate:

•	Has proven experience training adults in a professional or educational setting.

•	Has hands-on experience teaching or working with children between the ages of 5 and 14 years old.

•	Holds a background in coding, robotics, STEM education, or a closely related field.

•	Is comfortable using and teaching tools such as Scratch, Python, block-based coding platforms, or robotics kits (e.g., LEGO Mindstorms, mBot, or similar).

•	Demonstrates strong curriculum development and instructional design skills.

•	Is an excellent communicator — able to simplify complex technical concepts for varied audiences.

•	Is self-driven, organised, and able to work independently in a remote environment.

•	Is flexible and willing to travel when required.



Qualifications & Experience :



•	Diploma or Degree in Education, Computer Science, Engineering, or a related discipline.

•	Minimum 2 years of teaching or training experience — experience with both children and adults is strongly preferred.

•	Current or former school teachers specialising in Computing, ICT, STEM, or related subjects are warmly encouraged to apply.

•	Experience in curriculum writing, lesson planning, or instructional design is a strong advantage.

•	Experience in a franchising or licensee-based education business is a bonus, but not required.

•	Proficiency in digital tools for remote training delivery (e.g., Zoom, Google Meet, LMS platforms).



What We Offer :



•	Flexible, remote-based working arrangement.

•	A meaningful role shaping the next generation of coders and innovators.

•	A collaborative and supportive team environment.

•	Opportunities for professional development and growth within the organisation.

•	Travel allowances for required in-person training engagements.

•	Competitive remuneration commensurate with experience.

title: Part-Time Professional Video Editor (DaVinci Resolve / Premiere Pro) – Long Form Content
description:
We are looking for a highly skilled and experienced video editor to support an ongoing project with regular YouTube video uploads.



This is a part-time role to start, with the potential to grow into more work over time if the collaboration goes well.



Please read carefully before applying.





IMPORTANT REQUIREMENTS



You must be very experienced with professional editing software, specifically:



• DaVinci Resolve OR  

• Adobe Premiere Pro



Basic tools such as CapCut or similar apps are NOT sufficient for this role.



The footage is filmed in S-Log, so you must be very comfortable working with log footage and professional color grading, including using LUTs and building a strong grading workflow.



We are specifically looking for someone who has serious experience with professional editing and color grading — not simple social media edits or short-form content.





STYLE OF EDITING



The editing style is cinematic, calm, and high quality — not flashy or fast-paced.



The focus is on:



• Cinematic color grading  

• Careful attention to light, tone, and atmosphere  

• Occasional masking and professional grading techniques  

• Clean, thoughtful pacing



The videos are filmed with two static cameras on tripods, and the edit mainly involves switching between angles at the right 
nts while maintaining a cinematic feel.



The videos include peaceful background music, so it’s important that the editor has a good sense of rhythm and timing — knowing when to switch angles naturally with the flow of the video and music. Experience with properly syncing audio and video is also important.





PROJECT FORMAT



• Long-form YouTube videos (4K, horizontal), published weekly or bi-weekly  

• Editing focuses on creating a clean, cinematic long-form experience



Later on, after we are comfortable working together, we may also create short vertical clips or reels from the YouTube videos. However, the main focus is the long-form YouTube edits.





PLEASE ONLY APPLY IF



• You have years of experience with professional video editing  

• You are highly skilled at color grading  

• You have experience editing long-form horizontal content  

• You are comfortable working in 4K workflows with log footage

• You are confident using DaVinci Resolve or Premiere Pro at a professional level



If your experience is mainly short-form social media edits or simple Instagram content, this role will not be a good fit.





TO APPLY



Please send:



1. Your full name  

2. A short introduction  

3. Your editing software of choice (DaVinci or Premiere)  

4. Links to examples of your best work — especially long-form YouTube content  

5. Your hourly rate  

6. Your weekly availability



We’re looking forward to finding someone serious about their craft who enjoys creating beautiful cinematic edits.



Thank you.

title: Outsourced CFO / Controller — US Healthcare Company (Remote, Philippines)
description:
Job Title: Outsourced CFO / Controller — US Healthcare Company (Remote, Philippines)

Company: Remote Imaging Consultants (RIC)

Industry: Healthcare / Teleradiology (US-based)

Location: Remote — Philippines-based (Cebu preferred, Manila also welcome)

Engagement Type: Independent Contractor (No PH employment benefits)

Reports To: Company Owner (Physician-Entrepreneur, based in Hawaii)

Hours: Must be available during US business hours for key tasks (flexible schedule otherwise; Hawaii is UTC–10)

Equipment: Company-provided laptop + software licenses



ABOUT THE COMPANY

Remote Imaging Consultants (RIC) is a US-based teleradiology company providing radiology reading services to hospitals and imaging centers across the United States. We manage a network of radiologist contractors and develop proprietary cloud-based PACS and AI-powered clinical tools. We have operations in Hawaii with radiologist contractors in multiple US states, and a technology team in Taiwan and the Philippines.



RIC is currently structured as an S-Corporation and may transition to a different entity type (C-Corp or limited partnership) in the near future. We need someone who can grow with us through that transition.



ROLE OVERVIEW

We are looking for a skilled, reliable finance professional based in Cebu to serve as our outsourced CFO / Controller on a contractor basis. You will own the day-to-day financial operations of the company: bookkeeping, US tax preparation, financial reporting, and coordination with US-based tax professionals (CPA and/or tax attorney) for complex filings and entity planning.



This is a high-trust, high-autonomy role reporting directly to the owner. We want someone who is excellent at what they do, takes initiative, and communicates clearly.



KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

1) Bookkeeping and Accounting

- Maintain the company’s books using US accounting software (QuickBooks Online, Xero, or equivalent — open to your recommendation)

- Reconcile bank accounts, credit cards, and payment processor accounts monthly

- Process accounts payable and accounts receivable

- Track contractor payments (1099 recipients) and manage W-9 collection

- Manage payroll processing for US-based employees



2) US Tax Preparation and Compliance

- Prepare US federal and state corporate tax returns (currently Form 1120-S), or coordinate with a US-based CPA to ensure accurate and timely filing

- Prepare the owner’s personal federal and state tax returns (Form 1040), or coordinate preparation with a US-based CPA

- Calculate and file quarterly estimated tax payments (corporate and personal)

- Prepare and file 1099-NEC forms for all contractors annually

- Manage multi-state tax compliance: Hawaii (home state), Oregon (Corporate Activity Tax), California (franchise tax), and additional states as needed

- Coordinate with a US-based CPA or tax attorney for complex filings, IRS representation, and entity structuring as needed



3) Financial Reporting and Analysis

- Produce monthly financial statements (P&L, balance sheet, cash flow)

- Provide wRVU-based productivity and revenue analysis for radiologist contractors

- Develop and maintain financial dashboards and KPIs

- Support budgeting, forecasting, and cash flow planning



4) Strategic Support

- Advise on contractor compensation structures and client pricing strategy

- Support the owner and US-based tax professionals during any entity restructuring (S-Corp to C-Corp or LP, potential reincorporation in South Dakota or Delaware)

- Assist with financial aspects of new client contracts and negotiations



REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS

- Philippine CPA license (active and in good standing)

- Minimum 3 years of hands-on experience with US tax preparation and compliance (1120-S, 1040, state filings, 1099s)

- Proficient in US accounting software (QuickBooks Online, Xero, or equivalent)

- Previous experience working with US clients, either in a US-facing BPO or directly

- Strong understanding of US GAAP and IRS tax code as it applies to small businesses and S-Corporations

- Excellent English (written and verbal) — must communicate clearly with the owner, US attorneys, and financial institutions

- Reliable high-speed internet and a professional home office setup

- Based in the Philippines (Cebu preferred — the owner travels there regularly and other team members are based there, but Manila or elsewhere in the Philippines is fine for the right candidate)



PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

- US CPA license or US CPA exam passer (strongly preferred)

- PTIN (Preparer Tax Identification Number) holder

- Experience with healthcare or physician-owned businesses

- Familiarity with entity conversions (S-Corp ? C-Corp or LP)

- Experience coordinating with US tax attorneys on entity structuring

- International tax awareness (FBAR, FATCA, Form 2555, foreign income reporting)

- Big 4 or mid-tier BPO background (SGV/EY, Isla Lipana/PwC, or similar)

- Familiarity with 1099 contractor management and wRVU-based compensation models



COMPENSATION

This is an independent contractor engagement. No employment benefits (PhilHealth, SSS, Pag-IBIG, 13th month) are included — you are responsible for your own taxes and contributions.

- Monthly Rate: ?50,000 – ?80,000/month (~$860 – $1,400 USD), depending on experience

- Engagement Type: Independent contractor (no employment benefits)

- Schedule: Flexible; must overlap with US business hours for key tasks (Hawaii is UTC–10)

- Growth: Opportunity to grow into a strategic CFO role as the company scales



HOW TO APPLY

Send your resume/CV, a brief cover letter highlighting your US tax experience, and at least two professional references to BOTH:

- Joy (Recruitment Coordinator): 

- Henry (Operations):

title: Talent Acquisition Lead
description:
Build the Talent Engine Behind a High-Growth Financial Group



Location: Remote

Type: Full-time

Compensation: $3,000 per month AUD + performance-based bonus

Reports to: People & Culture Leader



This Is Not a Typical Recruiter Role



Aureus Financial is scaling across Education, Wealth, Accounting, and Growth Partner divisions. Our growth is only as strong as the people we bring in — and right now, hiring needs a dedicated owner.



We’re looking for a Talent Acquisition Lead to build and run a proactive A-player recruitment engine. This role is for someone who wants ownership, autonomy, and clear outcomes — not someone waiting for job briefs to land in their inbox.



If you’re metrics-driven, persuasive, and thrive on building pipelines before roles open, keep reading.



Your Mission



Build a predictable, high-quality pipeline of finance talent and run fast, structured hiring processes that deliver A-players — without slowing the business down.



Success is measured by speed, quality, and leverage created, not hours worked.



What You’ll Own



Talent Pipeline Development



Proactively source and engage A-player candidates across Education, Wealth, Accounting, and Growth Partner roles



Build and maintain segmented pipelines (ready-now vs future-fit)



Leverage outbound sourcing, referrals, and networks to reach passive talent



Anticipate hiring needs before roles are formally opened



Interview Process Ownership



Own recruitment end-to-end from sourcing through offer



Run structured screening interviews aligned to role scorecards



Drive interview velocity and keep candidates and hiring managers aligned



Deliver a high-quality, no-friction candidate experience



Employer Positioning & Network Expansion



Represent Aureus confidently in the finance talent market



Contribute to job ads, outreach messaging, and talent pitch materials



Build relationships in finance, accounting, and advisory communities



Improve outreach response rates and referral flow over time



Metrics & Optimisation



Track and report key recruitment metrics weekly



Understand your funnel: outreach ? screen ? interview ? offer



Identify bottlenecks and fix them without being asked



How You’ll Be Measured



Leading Indicators



30+ qualified candidates sourced per month



25%+ outbound response rate



10–15 structured screening interviews per month



Less than 5 days from application to first interview



Lagging Indicators



Average time-to-hire under 21 days



90%+ of hires rated 4 or 5 at 90-day review



80%+ offer acceptance rate



9/10 hiring manager satisfaction





This Role Is a Fit If You Are:

Proactive: You don’t wait for instructions — you build



Persuasive: You can engage and convert passive, high-performing candidates



Intrinsically Motivated: You take ownership of outcomes, not tasks



Metrics-Driven: You know your numbers and improve them



Direct and Organised: You keep processes tight and people aligned



Recruitment or talent acquisition experience is required, ideally in finance, accounting, or professional services environments.



This Role Is Not a Fit If You:

Prefer reactive, inbound-only recruiting



Need constant direction or micromanagement



Avoid accountability for outcomes



Are looking for a generalist HR role



Why Join Aureus Financial



Aureus Financial helps business owners build wealth, freedom, and certainty. We move fast, operate with high standards, and reward ownership and performance.



In this role, you will:



Work directly with senior leadership



Have autonomy to build systems that scale



Play a direct role in shaping the future team and culture



Be trusted to own outcomes, not just activity



How to Apply



Click here to apply: 



Please note that only those who have a VideoAsk submission will be entertained..

URGENT HIRING. MUST HAVE EXPERIENCED IN ECOMMERCE



Join our team and become part of a dynamic, global organization of 40 professionals across Korea, Singapore, Malaysia, and the Philippines. Collaborate closely with our regional teams to grow an exciting portfolio of leading Korean brands, including Beauty of Joseon, Needly, Tirtir, and other top-trending names in the market.

mber who can independently oversee e-commerce operations, take proactive initiative, and consistently deliver results with minimal supervision, ensuring long-term success and stability in the role.

- Highly organized, able to prioritize tasks and meet deadlines in a fast-paced environment.

- Strong communication skills with the ability to work effectively in multicultural teams.



Benefits:

title: Remote Estimating & Proposal Assistant (Construction / Fencing Industry)
description:
We are a New Zealand based fencing and construction company looking for a smart, detail-oriented Filipino tea
mber to join our business long term.



Your role will be to help turn customer enquiries and building plans into professional fencing proposals using our systems.



This is a stable, long-term remote position for someone who enjoys working with plans, measurements, and structured systems.



Your Main Responsibilities



You will help our team with:



• Reviewing architectural drawings and building plans

• Identifying fencing requirements from plans

• Setting up customers and projects in NetSuite

• Using our online 3D fence builder tools to design fences

• Preparing professional proposals/quotes in NetSuite

• Sending proposals to customers

• Organizing customer enquiries and project details



Some customers will be new enquiries, and some will be repeat clients.



Types of Fencing We Work With



Our company designs and supplies several fencing systems including:



• Glass pool fencing & balustrades

• Aluminium slat fencing & balustrades

• Solid wall fencing systems

• Acoustic fencing

• Pool fencing

• Architectural fencing systems



You will learn how to read plans and determine what fencing is required for each project.



The Person We Are Looking For



You must be:



? Very detail oriented

? Comfortable working with technical drawings or plans

? Organized and able to follow processes

? Good with online software and systems

? Reliable and able to work independently

? Good written English



Bonus skills (not required but helpful):



• Construction estimating

• Architecture or engineering background

• CAD experience

• Experience with NetSuite or ERP systems



Systems You Will Use



• NetSuite

• Online fence design tools

• Architectural plans and PDFs

•
 communication



Full training will be provided.



Application Instructions (IMPORTANT)



To apply, please include the following:



1?? A short video (1–2 minutes) introducing yourself

2?? Tell us about your work experience

3?? Explain why you believe you would be a good fit for this role

Please also explain how you would measure fencing from a site plan.



Applications without a video introduction will not be considered.



Bonus (Optional)



We will provide a sample proposal PDF so applicants can understand the type of work involved before starting.



Long Term Opportunity



We are looking for someone who wants to grow with our company long term.



If you are smart, reliable, and enjoy structured work, this could be a great opportunity.

title: Executive Assistant / Project Manager
description:
We’re hiring a highly organized, systems-minded Team Admin / Executive Assistant / Project Manager to support leadership and keep the team operating smoothly.



This role is for someone who thinks in systems, spots gaps before they become problems, stays ahead of deadlines, and keeps the entire team aligned without needing constant supervision. You are here to support leadership, manage projects, and keep systems running smoothly so the rest of the team can focus on execution.



—-----------------------------------------------------------------------------



READ THE ENTIRE JOB LISTING CAREFULLY. The final step of the hiring process will require the candidate to provide at least 1 former employer/team lead reference and at least 1 character reference. These will be verified prior to extending an offer.



--- ABOUT THE TEAM ---

Want to work in a rewarding, fast-paced, start-up atmosphere? Looking for significant growth and performance bonus opportunities? Have a growth mindset, value relationships, and dedicated to getting results? Then we might be a fit!



What is it like to work with us? HEAR FROM OUR TEAM!   





Our Core Values:

1) DELIGHT THE CLIENT:  We consistently exceed expectations by anticipating needs, delivering outstanding service, and creating those “magic 
nts” where every client feels seen and heard.

2) MASTER YOUR CRAFT:  We commit to continuous learning, share our expertise openly, and tackle every challenge with precision and a deep sense of duty.

3) FOLLOW THROUGH, ALWAYS:  When we say something will happen, it does—every time. We own every task, ensuring that nothing falls through the cracks, and when mistakes occur, we learn and improve immediately.



Our core values are important, and the right candidate will share our values. We are building an exceptional place to work with strong systems, high standards, and real impact, not just managing tasks for our real estate business.



--- ABOUT YOU ---

Do you think in priorities, timelines, and dependencies? Do you enjoy turning scattered ideas into structured plans with clear owners and deadlines? Are you the type who follows up before being asked and catches details others miss? Do you take pride in building clean systems, documenting processes properly, and keeping teams aligned without constant supervision?



Then keep reading to see if we're a fit!



RESPONSIBILITIES:

Admin & Team Support

- Manage calendars, meetings, follow-ups, and reminders

- Handle inbox organization and message triage

- Prepare documents, trackers, and internal updates

- Keep files, folders, and naming systems clean and consistent



Project Management

- Track ongoing projects, deadlines, and dependencies

- Break down tasks and assign owners

- Follow up on deliverables and flag risks early

- Keep project boards and trackers up to date (e.g. Notion, Monday, ClickUp, Sheets)



Systems & Process

- Build and improve simple systems that reduce back-and-forth

- Document SOPs and workflows as things evolve

- Spot inefficiencies and automation opportunities, and suggest, design, and implement improvements

- Maintain dashboards, trackers, and recurring reports



Communication & Coordination

- Act as the bridge between leadership and the team

- Clarify priorities and next steps after meetings

- Ensure instructions are understood and executed correctly

- Keep everyone aligned without micromanaging



REQUIREMENTS:

Experience & Capability

- Must have extensive experience as an executive assistant, chief of staff, or operations associate

- Project management experience and comfortable managing and driving multiple large projects

- Must have previous experience managing people. You may not have any direct reports, but the experience is important

- Must have experience automating workflows and designing workflow processes with automation in mind. You may not be individually responsible for building them, but you should already have the experience to handle basic tasks and understand how the process works

- Comfortable with outcome and goal-based delegation and does not need to be told individual tasks

- Experience with Google Suite and GoHighLevel CRM is a plus, but not required

- Should be fluent in AI, but not reliant on it to think for you



Work Style & Standards

- Extremely detail-oriented

- Excellent organizational skills

- Excellent time management skills

- Strong written communication

- Strong written and spoken English communication skills; near-native level preferred

- Comfortable juggling multiple priorities

- Must love creating, refining, and working with standard operating processes (SOPs)

- Proactive. You don’t wait to be told something is broken, you anticipate it before it breaks

- Must move with a sense of speed and urgency in everything



Ownership & Mindset

- Extremely growth-oriented and hungry to learn

- You are the type of person who follows through every single time, no matter what, and always gets the job done. No excuses, no matter what pops up

- You are the type of person who is delighted when the client or the person you're preparing something for is delighted

- You want meaningful work around people who are incredibly smart, hard-working, and dedicated, with a phenomenal culture. We work hard, but we have fun doing it together



Availability & Technical Requirements

- A STABLE, HIGH-SPEED INTERNET CONNECTION IS REQUIRED (at least 150mbps)

- Must be willing to work long hours and be available to respond to messages within a 12-hour daily window (not necessarily working 12 hours a day, but reliably reachable and able to respond quickly if something urgent comes up)



Preferred:

- Experience working as a process engineer, manufacturing engineer, or industrial engineer is preferred, but not required

- Experience designing team workflows, augmenting existing workflows with automation, and/or automating existing workflows is a strong bonus, but not required



--- APPLICATION & RECRUITMENT PROCESS ---

STEP 1:

Fill out the application form through the link below and write "green kangaroo" in the Additional Comments field.





STEP 2:

Qualified candidates may be invited to participate in two PAID test projects, $10-20 USD for each test project, estimated to be 2-3 hours of work for each (a total of $20-40 USD!).



STEP 3:

Candidates who advance through the test project rounds will be invited to an interview.



STEP 4:

Before extending a trial offer, we will conduct a CHARACTER REFERENCE CHECK for the final candidate(s). CONDUCTING A REFERENCE CHECK will be the last step in the hiring process.



STEP 5:

Upon receiving a trial offer, you will be onboarded as a Team Admin / Assistant / Project Manager with a 90-day PAID trial period. During this period, we expect you to demonstrate your video editing expertise, meet project deadlines, be a team player, and excel at what you do. A successful trial will lead to a permanent offer to join the team.

title: SHORT FORM VIDEO EDITOR | Flexible Work Schedule
description:
We’re hiring someone who knows how to turn long-form content into sharp, high-retention short videos for platforms like TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts.



This role is for someone who understands pacing, hooks, and visual rhythm in short-form content, and knows how to edit with intention rather than just cutting clips.



—-----------------------------------------------------------------------------



READ THE ENTIRE JOB LISTING CAREFULLY. The final step of the hiring process will require the candidate to provide at least 1 former employer/team lead reference and at least 1 character reference. These will be verified prior to extending an offer.



--- ABOUT THE TEAM ---

Want to work in a rewarding, fast-paced, start-up atmosphere? Looking for significant growth and performance bonus opportunities? Have a growth mindset, value relationships, and dedicated to getting results? Then we might be a fit!



What is it like to work with us? HEAR FROM OUR TEAM!   





Our Core Values:

1) DELIGHT THE CLIENT:  We consistently exceed expectations by anticipating needs, delivering outstanding service, and creating those “magic 
nts” where every client feels seen and heard.

2) MASTER YOUR CRAFT:  We commit to continuous learning, share our expertise openly, and tackle every challenge with precision and a deep sense of duty.

3) FOLLOW THROUGH, ALWAYS:  When we say something will happen, it does—every time. We own every task, ensuring that nothing falls through the cracks, and when mistakes occur, we learn and improve immediately.



Our core values are important and the right candidate will share our values. We are building an awesome place to work, not just cutting together videos for our real estate business.



--- ABOUT YOU ---

Do you have strong creative instincts and know how to spot 
nts that make people stop scrolling? Are you proactive, adaptable, and detail-oriented? Do you have a sharp sense of pacing, an instinct for hooks, and a clear understanding of how short-form videos need to flow to hold attention? Do you take pride in clean edits and delivering videos that feel intentional and finished? Do our core values sound like a fit for who you are?



Then keep reading to see if we're a fit!



Responsibilities:

Short-Form Editing & Story Selection

- Review long-form videos, transcripts, or raw footage to identify short-form clip opportunities

- Select 
nts that are clear, engaging, and platform-appropriate

- Edit vertical videos optimized for TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts

- Create strong hooks, clean cuts, and natural pacing

- Deliver publish-ready videos that meet brand and platform standards



Captions, Visuals & Formatting

- Add on-screen captions (burned-in or SRT as required) with proper timing and readability

- Apply basic motion, zooms, punch-ins, and visual emphasis where appropriate

- Follow platform-specific formatting (aspect ratio, duration, safe margins)



Iteration & Workflow

- Implement feedback efficiently and meet agreed turnaround times

- Maintain clear file naming and folder organization

- Track edits, revisions, and delivery status as required



Requirements:

- A STABLE, HIGH-SPEED INTERNET CONNECTION, REQUIRED (at least 150mbps)

- Experience with Final Cut Pro X (or other editing software, such as Adobe After Effects and Premiere Pro) is required

- Strong preference for MacOS users

- Experience editing short-form vertical content for social platforms, required

- Strong understanding of short-form pacing, hooks, and retention

- Experience working from transcripts or long-form content, required

- Clean file organization and understanding of post-production workflo

- Portfolio of completed short-form video projects, required

- General understanding of brand, content marketing, YouTube, etc, required

- GREAT interpersonal and communication skills, required

- Ability to self-start, work on a tight schedule, and manage multiple project deadlines, required

- Ability to follow directions and a LOVE of completing lots of projects according to consistent specifications, required

- Growth mindset, required



--- APPLICATION & RECRUITMENT PROCESS ---

STEP 1:

Fill out the application form through the link below and write "green kangaroo" in the Additional Comments field.


 



STEP 2:

Qualified candidates may be invited to participate in two PAID test projects, $10-20 USD for each test project estimated to be 2-3hrs of work for each (a total of $40 USD!).



STEP 3:

Candidates who advance through the test project rounds will be invited to an interview.



STEP 4:

Before extending a trial offer, we will conduct a CHARACTER REFERENCE CHECK for the final candidate(s). CONDUCTING A REFERENCE CHECK will be the last step in the hiring process.



STEP 5:

Upon receiving a trial offer, you will be onboarded as a Video Editor with a 90-day PAID trial period. During this period, we expect you to demonstrate your video editing expertise, meet project deadlines, be a team player, and excel at what you do. A successful trial will lead to a permanent offer to join the team.

title: EDITOR/SOCIAL MEDIA MANAGER
description:
About



MAXIMUM is a faith-driven video marketing and event production company and a movement. We are a one-stop shop for founders and thought leaders, producing founder films, short documentaries, cinematic shortform, podcasts, video ads, keynote capture, testimonials, and event after-movies. We design end-to-end event experiences for high-ticket conferences, summits, and masterminds, then repurpose every asset across YouTube, Shorts, Reels, TikTok, and LinkedIn to grow authority and revenue.



Mission



Help impactful entrepreneurs reach their Maximum potential by clarifying their message, crafting world-class video around it, and distributing it at scale so it reaches millions, resonates deeply, and creates real change.



Vision



Serve the world’s greatest change-makers and the next generation of transformational voices and amplify their message with cinematic precision and cultural excellence. Build a flagship MAXIMUM Arena and Campus and a global MAXIMUM circuit from Miami to Dubai, London to Singapore. Operate as a Studio and an Event Space that create movements, communities, and deal flow, with the long-term goal of impacting 100 million lives.









We are looking for a hungry, creative, and reliable Video Editor / Content Manager who wants to grow with a fast-moving company and become a key part of a long-term team.



This is more than a basic freelance editing role. We are looking for someone who wants to build with us, sharpen their craft, and help us create high-quality content for high-level entrepreneurs and brands.



Role Overview



You will be responsible for editing and packaging content across multiple formats, helping maintain a consistent creative standard, and supporting the overall content workflow from post-production to final delivery.



This role starts primarily focused on editing, but the right person should also be able to support with thumbnails, posting, and content organization, with room to grow into a larger content management role over time.



Responsibilities

Video Editing



Edit short form content for Instagram, TikTok, YouTube Shorts, and other social platforms

title: $$Rare Opportunity $$ GoHighLevel Manager / Automation Needed
description:
I have the licenses, you grow it to make the money and share in the success!



We’re looking for a self driven GoHighLevel (GHL) admin who doesn’t just “run” the platform—they *weaponize* it. If you live in the backend, think in funnels, automate everything that can be automated, and naturally engineer high converting systems without waiting to be told what to do, this is your spot.



You’ll be responsible for growing our GoHighLevel license as its own profit center—an entire money making department inside the agency. You’ll:

- Build, scale, and optimize full stack client systems: funnels, automations, text/email campaigns, SMS, calendars, and business workflows.

- Architect GoHighLevel stacks that close leads, reduce friction, and increase LTV for local service businesses.

- Use your own creativity—not canned templates—to design unique, high performance campaigns that actually sell.

- Audit, streamline, and systemize existing client setups so we can clone what works across clients.

- Work with minimal oversight: you’ll be trusted to steer the ship, make decisions, and own the results.



### **What We’re Looking For**

- Proven, hands on experience as a **GoHighLevel admin, operator, or systems architect**.

- Deep understanding of funnel psychology and how backend systems drive frontend sales.

- You don’t wait for a “brief” or “creative brief”—you *are* the creative and the architect.

- Strong organizational sense and ability to document systems so others can scale them.

- Comfortable working asynchronously with minimal micromanagement.

- Bonus if you have experience with:

  - Local service businesses (HVAC, roofing, solar, contractors, etc.)

  - White label agency setups

  - Lead gen funnels, SMS nurturing, and calendar based conversion workflows.



### **What You’re Not**

- You’re not just a “click around” admin.  

- You’re not waiting for a creative director to point you to a template.  

- You’re not someone who needs step by step instructions for every campaign.



You’re the person who looks at a blank GHL account and sees **a fully fledged revenue engine**—and then builds it.



### **What You Get**

- A high impact role in a fast moving agency where you directly control profit via systems.

- Autonomy to design, test, and scale your own ideas.

- Opportunity to turn **one GoHighLevel license into a repeatable, scalable department across multiple clients.

- Performance based incentives and growth opportunities tied to revenue you help generate.



If you’re the kind of GHL operator who thrives on freedom, systems, and results—and you’re ready to steer the ship instead of just follow the map—send us:

- A short note showing that you *get* what we’re after (no generic copy paste).

- 1–2 examples of funnels or systems you’ve built in Go High Level (screenshots or links).

- Your preferred way to work (remote, hours, time zone, etc.).



Send me your resume and a loom video introducing yourself and your skillset and success stories with the platform to

We are a one-stop shop for founders and thought leaders, producing founder films, short documentaries, cinematic shortform, podcasts, video ads, keynote capture, testimonials, and event after-movies. We design end-to-end event experiences for high-ticket conferences, summits, and masterminds, then repurpose every asset across YouTube, Shorts, Reels, TikTok, and LinkedIn to grow authority and revenue.
