title: Outbound Appointment Setter – Cold Calls to US Business Owners
description:
Outbound Appointment Setter – Cold Calls to US Business Owners

Remote | Performance Bonuses | Long-Term Opportunity



We are hiring an expierenced appointment setter to contact service business owners in the United States and schedule product demonstrations.



You will be speaking directly with US business owners, so excellent spoken English and the ability to communicate clearly with US business owners is required.



Your responsibility is setting demo appointments only.

You will not run demos or close sales.



Once an appointment is scheduled, a sales closer runs the 15–20 minute demo and handles the sale.



What You Will Do

• Make 80–120 outbound cold calls per day

• Speak with US business owners

• Identify the correct decision maker

• Schedule 15–20 minute demo appointments with our closer

• Transfer calls live to the closer when available

• Send short follow-up
s when requested by gatekeepers

• Follow up on those
s with additional calls



Leads are provided.



You will be calling from a prepared list of businesses. You do not need to research or find leads.



Calls are made using our system in GoHighLevel.

A phone number and
 address will be provided.



Businesses are called during their normal operating hours (9am–5pm local US business time).



This role is primarily phone-based outbound calling.



What You Will NOT Do

You will NOT:

• close deals

• run product demos

• configure software

• provide technical support



Your only responsibility is setting qualified demo appointments.



Pay Structure

You will earn:

• $5 for every qualified demo appointment booked

• $100 bonus if the deal closes from your appointment



A qualified demo means the business owner attends the scheduled demo with our closer.



The more qualified appointments you schedule, the more you earn.



Daily Activity Target

Successful tea
mbers typically complete 80–120 outbound calls per day while scheduling demo appointments.

This role is best suited for people who enjoy high-activity phone work.



Requirements

• Previous cold calling or appointment setting experience is required

• Excellent spoken English and ability to communicate clearly with US business owners

• Comfortable making 80–120 calls per day

• Reliable internet connection

• Quiet working environment

• Confidence speaking on the phone

Candidates with call center or BPO outbound sales experience are strongly preferred.



Do NOT Apply If

• You are not comfortable making 80–120 calls per day

• You prefer chat,
, or non-phone work

• You do not have previous cold calling experience



Application Instructions

To apply, send:

1.Voice Recording (30 seconds)

Please record yourself saying the following exactly as written:

“Hi, this is [your name]. I saw you may be hiring someone to help with calls or scheduling, and I wanted to ask a quick question about how you’re currently handling incoming calls when things get busy.”

Speak naturally as if you are calling a US business owner.



Do not read this slowly like a script. Speak as if you are on a real call.

Upload the recording with your application.



2. Answer These Questions

a) What was the highest number of calls you made in a single day at a previous job?

b) Tell us about the last job where you had to make a large number of calls every day.

c) If asked to make 100 calls per day, would that feel normal or stressful for you?



Important

To confirm you read this job post, start your application message with the word:

APPOINTMENT



Applications without the voice recording will not be reviewed.



Work Environment

• Fully remote

• Work from home

• Long-term opportunity with a growing company

We are building a stable long-term remote team.

title: Construction Operations & Finance Analyst
description:
Lakestone Partners is a growing U.S. real estate and construction company. We manage property renovations, maintenance operations, and real estate projects.



We are hiring a Construction Operations & Finance Analyst to help manage construction financial tracking, project coordination, and internal systems.



This role supports our Director of Construction and leadership team by ensuring projects stay organized, profitable, and on schedule.



You will help manage project budgets, invoicing, reporting, and build systems using QuickBooks and ClickUp.



This is a long-term remote position for candidates based in the Philippines.

Construction Financial Tracking



Draft and prepare construction quotes and estimates



Track job budgets and actual project costs



Monitor project profitability



Manage construction expenses inside QuickBooks



Track vendor invoices and payments



Maintain accurate job costing data

Create workflows for construction and maintenance jobs



Track project progress and deadlines



Coordinate schedules with contractors and vendors



Maintain project documentation

Generate invoices for construction projects



Track outstanding invoices



Follow up on unpaid invoices



Maintain accurate billing records



Reporting



Prepare weekly reports including:



Job budgets vs actual spending



Project profitability



Outstanding invoices



Construction project status



Executive Support



Manage the Director of Construction’s calendar



Schedule vendor meetings



Track important deadlines



Coordinate project updates



Required Skills



Strong QuickBooks experience



Experience with job costing or project accounting



Strong Excel or Google Sheets skills



Excellent written English



Strong organization and attention to detail



Ability to manage multiple projects simultaneously



Preferred Experience



Experience working with construction companies



Experience working with U.S. companies



Familiarity with ClickUp or project management systems



Experience with renovation or construction project tracking



Software Used



You will work with:



QuickBooks



ClickUp



Google Workspace



Project reporting tools



Work Schedule



Full-time



Remote



Must be comfortable working partially within U.S. business hours



Compensation



Depending on experience:



This is a long-term position with growth opportunities.



Application Instructions (Important)



To apply for this position:



Begin your application with the phrase:



“Lakestone Construction Systems”



Answer the following questions:



What QuickBooks reports have you used to track job profitability?



Have you worked with construction or renovation companies before?



What project management systems have you used (ClickUp, Asana, Monday, etc.)?



Briefly explain how you would track a renovation budget.



Include a short summary of your QuickBooks experience.



Applications that do not follow these instructions will not be reviewed.



Short Skills Test (Required)



Please answer the following:



Project Budget: $40,000



Electrical Budget: $6,000

Plumbing Budget: $4,500

Kitchen Budget: $12,000

Flooring Budget: $7,000

Paint Budget: $3,500

Misc Budget: $7,000



Invoices Received:



Electrical: $6,800

Plumbing: $3,900

Kitchen: $10,500

Flooring: $6,200

Paint: $3,900

Misc: $2,100



Questions:



Which categories are over budget?



What is the total spent so far?



What remaining budget is left?



Why This Role Matters



This position helps ensure that construction projects stay organized, profitable, and properly tracked.



You will play a key role in improving systems and helping our company scale efficiently.

title: Senior Media Buyer / Strategist
description:
READ CAREFULLY — This is NOT a button-pushing job.



We're a U.S.-based creative and advertising agency with a big mission and a lean, tight-knit team. We help personal injury lawyers across all 50 states generate cases consistently, scale their practices, and dominate their local markets. We script the content. We create the ads. We build the strategy. We optimize everything. And we win.



We're looking for a senior-level, full-stack media buyer and strategist who doesn't just run ads — you THINK like a marketer. You research the market. You study the competition. You script the messaging. You build the campaigns. And then you optimize them until they print results.



About the Role



As our lead media buyer and strategist, you'll own the entire paid advertising process from research to results. You'll collaborate directly with our small, highly engaged team — we operate like a family, and your input will be heard, respected, and valued.



You'll be in constant communication with our creative team, project leads, and founder. There's plenty of direction and clarity — but you'll also have the freedom to bring strategy, ideas, and initiative to the table. We want someone who takes ownership, not someone who waits to be told what to do.



Our Vision



We're building the most effective advertising system for law firms in the country. We believe in long-term brand strategy, not gimmicks. Paid media is the engine that drives revenue for our clients — the campaigns you build will directly impact real businesses and real people. If that excites you, we want you on this team.



What You'll Be Doing



- Conduct deep market research and competitor analysis to guide ad strategy and creative direction for each client and market

- Script ad content (video and static) — you'll write the hooks, the angles, the messaging frameworks that drive results

- Build, launch, and optimize Meta 
/Instagram) ad campaigns end-to-end — this is the core of the role

- Manage ad budgets strategically, scaling what works and killing what doesn't — fast

- Set up and manage lead flows through Zapier and GoHighLevel (GHL), ensuring leads are pushed into client CRMs accurately

- Develop and execute A/B testing strategies across creatives, copy, audiences, and placements

- Analyze campaign performance data and translate it into clear, actionable insights and next steps

- Collaborate with our creative team on ad concepts, providing strategic briefs that guide design and video production

- Stay current on platform changes, algorithm updates, and emerging ad strategies — and bring new ideas proactively



Tools & Communication



- Meta Ads Manager (Business Suite) — this is your primary workspace

- Zapier — for automating lead delivery and workflows

- GoHighLevel (GHL) — our CRM platform for client lead management

- Google Sheets / Docs — for reporting, scripting, and strategy documentation

- 
 — for daily communication

- Quick feedback loops and open communication — we move fast, but respectfully



Requirements



- 5+ years of hands-on experience running paid social campaigns (Meta is non-negotiable)

- Proven ability to conduct market research, competitor analysis, and translate findings into ad strategy

- Strong copywriting and scripting skills — you can write ad scripts, hooks, and angles that convert

- Deep understanding of direct-response marketing AND brand awareness strategy

- Experience managing $100K+ monthly ad budgets across multiple accounts

- Proficiency with Zapier, GoHighLevel, and CRM-based lead management workflows

- Data-driven mindset — you make decisions based on numbers, not feelings

- Extremely detail-oriented and self-managed — you hit deadlines with zero micromanagement

- Reliable internet and full-time availability (40 hours/week, U.S. Eastern hours)



Bonus Points If You



- Have experience running ads for law firms, medical practices, or local service businesses

- Can run campaigns on Google Ads, YouTube, and/or TikTok in addition to Meta

- Have experience with creative strategy frameworks (e.g., problem–agitate–solve, hook frameworks)

- Have worked with U.S.-based marketing agencies before

- Understand the legal marketing landscape and compliance considerations

- Are self-motivated and take pride in driving measurable results



Compensation: Based on experience. Performance bonuses available for exceptional results. We reward people who move the needle. This can be a life-changing position for the right candidate. 



****HOW TO APPLY (This Is Your First Test)****



Do NOT apply through the job board.
 us directly at: 



Use the subject line: "Sharp Strategist – [Your Name]"



In your
, include:



- A brief summary (3–5 sentences) of a campaign you ran that you're most proud of — include the niche, budget, strategy, and results

- One example of a market or competitor research process you've used to inform an ad campaign

- A sample ad script or hook you've written (for video or static — just show us how you think)

- What ad platforms you have experience with and your strongest one

- How many ad accounts you can confidently manage simultaneously

- Your monthly salary expectation (full-time, USD)



If your background looks like a match, we'll schedule a 10-minute intro call and send a small paid test task.



We're serious about building a long-term team with people who think strategically, execute relentlessly, and take pride in driving real results. We don't want someone who just "runs ads." We want a marketer who happens to be deadly with a media budget.



If that's you — we can't wait to meet you.

title: THE BEST COMPANY TO WORK FOR is Hiring A Superstar Dispatcher.
description:
WE’RE HIRING: Dispatcher for Non-Emergency Transportation (CST Hours)




 is a fast-growing transportation tech company, and we’re looking for ONE exceptional Dispatcher to join our newest venture.



This role is not for everyone. It’s high-pressure, fast-paced, and phone-heavy. We’re looking for someone who can stay calm, focused, and effective while handling a high volume of calls and multiple moving parts at once.



SCHEDULE

Monday–Friday

8:00 AM – 4:00 PM (Chicago / CST)

Full-time only



You must be able to commit to this schedule consistently.



WHAT YOU’LL BE DOING

Dispatching drivers and coordinating non-emergency transportation rides.

Handling a high volume of inbound and outbound calls.

Communicating constantly with drivers and customers.

Solving problems in real time and making fast, accurate decisions.

Managing routes, traffic, and logistics using Google Maps and Google Sheets.

Multitasking under pressure while staying organized and professional.



This position has long-term growth potential for the right person.



THIS JOB IS FOR YOU IF YOU

Thrive in stressful, fast-moving environments.

Can juggle multiple tasks without dropping the ball.

Are comfortable being on the phone most of the day.

Take ownership and don’t make excuses.



This job is for the few who have what it takes to excel.



MUST-HAVES (NON-NEGOTIABLE)

Excellent English, spoken and written, near-native level.

Experience in dispatching, logistics, transportation, or a call center is strongly preferred.

Reliable computer and fast, stable internet connection.

Stable power source with no rolling outages. Be honest about this — power issues can lead to job loss.

Strong multitasking and communication skills.

Comfortable using Google Sheets, Google Maps, and other online tools.

Solid knowledge of U.S. roads, traffic patterns, and routing.



COMPENSATION

Starting at $800 per month.

Performance-based bonuses.

Clear growth opportunities for top performers.



HOW TO APPLY — READ CAREFULLY

Failure to follow instructions will result in automatic rejection.



Step 1

Modify your resume. At the very top, write exactly:

“I AM AWESOME AND I WILL ABSOLUTELY ROCK THIS POSITION.”

Yes, in ALL CAPS.



Step 2

 your resume to 



Subject line: DISPATCHER APPLICATION – I’M READY



Step 3

Include a one-minute video introduction of yourself in the
.



Video is mandatory. No video means we will not review your application. Following directions matters.



We’re fun, fast, and serious about building something great. If you take charge, stay sharp under pressure, and deliver results — we want you.





Not your average tech company. We move fast, expect a lot, and reward excellence.

title: Virtual Assistant - Podcast Production & Content Operations
description:
Part-time | Remote | 5-10 hrs/week to start | Growth opportunity



About the Role

I'm Alan Regala, founder of Athlete to Owner (
) and host of The Franchise Champion Show podcast. I help professionals transition from corporate careers into franchise ownership.

I'm looking for a detail-oriented, proactive VA to take my podcast production and content workflow off my plate so I can focus on coaching, interviews, and growing the business.

This starts at 5-10 hours per week. As you learn the systems and demonstrate good work, hours will grow. The right person could eventually become a key part of a small team.



What You'll Be Doing

Podcast production (core of the role):

After each interview recording, you'll take the transcript and work with AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude, etc.) to produce show notes, a blog post, a YouTube description with chapters, and LinkedIn post drafts. You'll follow established templates and style guidelines. You'll coordinate with the video editor, send him what he needs, and manage that back-and-forth. Once the episode is finalized, you'll handle publishing to YouTube, Spotify, Apple Podcasts, and LinkedIn. You'll also research and reach out to potential podcast guests, track responses, and schedule pre-interview prep calls.

Research and client support:

Look up franchise information, compile notes, and help prepare presentation slides for client meetings. I'll give you clear direction on what I need. I just need someone to handle the execution.



What I'm Looking For

Strong written English is the most important thing. You'll be drafting content that represents my voice and brand. Readers should not be able to tell it wasn't written by me.

Reliability and communication come next. I need someone who replies promptly, flags problems early, and follows through without being chased.

AI proficiency is essential. You'll be using ChatGPT and similar tools daily. You won't be creating everything from scratch; you'll be directing AI and refining the output.

Podcast or content experience is a plus, but not required if you're a fast learner and take good notes.



Skills Required

Excellent written English

Strong communicator; responsive and proactive

Proficient with ChatGPT or similar AI tools

Comfortable with Google Docs, Google Slides, and basic research

Able to follow detailed templates and style guides



Nice to Have

Experience with podcast workflows or content production

Familiarity with YouTube Studio, Spotify for Podcasters, or LinkedIn

Experience with Canva



Hours and Pay

Starting at 5-10 hours per week, part-time

Clear path to grow to 20+ hours as trust is built

Rate: $4-6/hr



How to Apply

Please record a short Loom video (90 seconds or less) and include the link in your application. In your video, cover these three things:

- Introduce yourself and describe your relevant experience

- Give one example of a time you managed a multi-step process or project

- What do you think is the most important thing when communicating with someone you're supporting?



Note: Applications without a Loom video will not be considered. This is the single best way for me to get a sense of who you are and how you communicate.

title: Virtual Assistant (STR Guest Support + Maintenance Coordination)
description:
Role Summary



This role supports daily operations for a short-term rental management business. It requires organization, precision, accuracy, and strong time management. While there may be occasional slow periods, this is not a “chill” job. The company’s reputation is at stake in every guest interaction, message, and phone call. This position is for someone who can operate autonomously, think critically, and consistently protect the guest experience to earn 5-star reviews.



Availibility

We are looking to have a shift from 4pm-8pm PST and possibly later. Monday and Friday.

We are open to having shifts over the weekend as well depending on availibility



Compensation

Compensation will start at $4 an hour. We are quickly looking to raise to $4.50 an hour based on performance. Performance will be assessed again in 6-12 months for an additional raise to $5.00



Core Responsibilities

1) Guest Messaging + Customer Service (Primary)



Monitor and respond to guest inquiries and active-stay messages with a professional, upbeat tone.



Create real human interactions through the app—guests should feel supported, not automated.



De-escalate issues calmly, set expectations, and drive solutions that protect guest satisfaction and reviews.



Prioritize messages correctly (urgent vs. non-urgent) and escalate only when required by SOP.



2) Maintenance & Issue Resolution (End-to-End Ownership)



You will fully own the maintenance workflow without requiring additional internal staffing or owner attention:



Receive guest maintenance requests and immediately log them in our task software with complete details.



Schedule the correct maintenance vendor/cleaning tea
mber, confirm availability, and confirm appointment time with the guest.



Follow up with maintenance tea
mbers before arrival and ensure they understand requirements.



Require photo documentation before/after (or proof of completion) and ensure the task is closed properly.



Communicate proactively with guests throughout so the process runs autonomously.



Sloppy scheduling, lack of follow-up, and missed coordination will not be tolerated.



3) Phone Calls (Required)



Call guests when appropriate (urgent issues, clarifications, service recovery).



Call maintenance tea
mbers to schedule, confirm, and resolve issues.



Messaging/texting is a large part of the job, but texting-only is not allowed.



4) Message Monitoring Standards (Non-Negotiable)



Messages must be checked every 30 minutes during your shift (set an alarm if needed).



New inquiries should ideally receive a response within 10 minutes.



Ghosting guests for hours is unacceptable.



Required Experience



Prior work experience supporting Airbnb operations.



Supervisor-level experience preferred (or equivalent responsibility managing escalations and team coordination).



Strong ability to multitask across multiple conversations, issues, and timelines.



Required Skills & Traits



Highly organized, detail-oriented, and reliable with time management.



Clear, upbeat, professional communication style (written and verbal).



Critical thinking: ability to filter low-importance messages from high-importance issues and act accordingly.



Autonomous execution: learns quickly, follows SOPs, and handles issues end-to-end without reminders.



Customer-experience mindset: the goal is not just completing tasks—it’s ensuring guests feel cared for and leave 5-star reviews.



Tools / Tech Requirements



Reliable high-speed internet (required).



Comfortable working across Airbnb, task software, and communication tools simultaneously.



Ability to maintain organized notes, task logs, and timelines.



Reliability & Attendance Policy (Strict)



No-shows without legitimate, valid reasons will result in immediate termination.



Reliability is mandatory. This role directly impacts guest experience and business reputation.



Schedule / Coverage



Shift-based coverage (details provided during hiring).



Must maintain the 30-minute message-check standard for the full shift.



How to Apply



Please submit:



A brief summary of your Airbnb support experience (include number of listings supported and responsibilities).



Any supervisor/lead experience (if applicable).



Your availability and time zone.



A short example of how you would respond (tone + action plan) to:



A guest saying Wi-Fi isn’t working, and



A guest requesting urgent plumbing help.



If you want, I can also add a short paid trial task and a scorecard (response speed, tone, task accuracy, follow-through) to screen candidates.

title: Full-Stack Developer / AI-Powered Virtual Assistant (Automations + Web + Agentic Workflows)
description:
WHO WE ARE



Arclight Content is an AI-powered production and technology company — and we're just getting started. In addition to Arclight, we operate and control several rapidly growing companies including a real estate AI platform, medical clinics, addiction treatment facilities, cannabis consulting operations, and more. Every company we run is being rebuilt with AI at the core.



We are a startup in the truest sense — fast, lean, ambitious, and early. That matters because the person we hire now is not just filling a role. They are getting in early on something real, with real upside, real growth, and a clear path to more responsibility and more money as we scale.



---



THE OPPORTUNITY



This is a full-time, long-term position with consistent pay and consistent work. We are not a gig. We are not a short-term contract. We want someone who wants to grow with us.



Here's what that path looks like:



? Start by owning our technical build and automation backlog

? Demonstrate you can ship and manage projects independently

? Grow into a management role with junior assistants reporting to you

? Be part of the decision-making as we scale across our portfolio of companies



If you help us build this efficiently, you will be rewarded for it. We take care of people who perform.



---



WHAT THE ROLE ACTUALLY IS



Think: Full-Stack Developer meets AI-Powered Virtual Assistant.



This is not a writing job. This is not a "use ChatGPT to summarize
s" job. We need someone who uses AI the way a developer does — to build things, automate things, and solve problems faster than any single person could alone.



You will be responsible for:



DEVELOPMENT & WEB



- Building professional websites (we prefer Elementor on WordPress but are open to modern alternatives — show us what you can do)

- Front-end and back-end work: HTML, CSS, JavaScript, PHP, Python — doesn't have to be deep, but you need to read and write code with AI assistance confidently

- Integrating third-party APIs into websites and tools

- Managing domains, hosting, deployment (Vercel, cPanel, Cloudflare, etc.)



AUTOMATION & AI WORKFLOWS



- Building agentic AI workflows using tools like n8n, Make, or Zapier integrated with AI models

- Automating tasks that used to require a human: transcription pipelines, content routing, lead processing, inbox management, report generation, scheduling

- Connecting AI models (Claude, Gemini, Kimi, GLM, Minimax) into functional tools and workflows

- Using Claude Code, Cursor, or similar AI coding assistants to build faster



VIRTUAL ASSISTANT / OPERATIONS



- Managing content posting, scheduling, and basic inbox triage

- Light research and competitive analysis (AI-assisted)

- Keeping projects organized across multiple brands

- Helping onboard and eventually delegate to junior virtual assistants



---



WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR



Must Have:



? Portfolio showing websites you personally built — Elementor experience a strong plus

? Real experience using Claude Code, Cursor, or an AI coding assistant to BUILD something — not just generate content

? Ability to build and connect automations in n8n, Make, or Zapier with API integrations

? Comfortable with Python, JavaScript, or PHP (read, edit, and ship with AI help)

? Self-directed — you get a brief and you figure out the rest

? Strong written English communication

? Available for overlap with Eastern US time zone (EST)



Strong Bonus:



? Experience with Supabase, Vercel, or similar backend/hosting platforms

? Familiarity with Next.js or React

? GitHub / version control experience

? SEO knowledge for web builds

? Experience building AI-powered tools or dashboards

? Multi-model AI experience (using Claude + Gemini + Kimi together, etc.)

? Any experience in medical, real estate, or regulated industries



---



WHAT WE OFFER



???? Access to the best AI tools available — we will provide you Claude Max, Gemini, Kimi K2.5, GLM, Minimax, and more. You will have access to infrastructure most freelancers pay hundreds per month out of pocket for.

???? $800–$2,500/month based on your actual skill level — with clear upside as we grow

???? Real promotion path — management role with delegation authority is on the table for the right person

???? 100% remote, flexible, results-driven culture

???? Long-term relationship — consistent work, consistent pay, consistent respect



---



A NOTE ON YOUR APPLICATION



We use AI tools every single day. You are absolutely welcome and encouraged to use AI to help you write and organize your application.



However — we have interviewed hundreds of applicants. We know exactly what ChatGPT gives when you ask it "how do I answer this job application question." We know what Claude outputs for "write me a response about my automation experience."



If your entire application reads like a generic AI response with no personal specifics — no real project names, no real tools, no real numbers, no actual experiences — we will know immediately and your application will not move forward.



Use AI to polish. Use your own brain to provide the substance. That is exactly the skill we are hiring for.



---



HOW TO APPLY



Every application must include ALL of the following or it will not be reviewed:



1. Links to 2–3 websites you personally built or significantly developed

2. Describe one automation or agentic workflow you have built — what it does, what tools you used, what problem it solved

3. Tell us specifically how you have used Claude Code, Cursor, or an AI coding assistant to build something real (be specific — tool, project, outcome)

4. Your current AI tool stack — which models and tools do you use daily and for what?

5. Your available hours and time zone

title: AI-Powered Operations Coordinator / Executive Support
description:
Job Title

AI-Powered Operations Coordinator / Executive Support

Remote  |  Long-Term  |  Part-Time to Full-Time



Who We Are and How We Work

I run multiple businesses from Florida in the United States, including architectural design, real estate, and an emerging voice AI agency. My team is small but growing, with members across the U.S., India, Pakistan, Ethiopia, and the Philippines. We are building something together, and the culture matters just as much as the work.



Before you read the job description, I want you to know what kind of team you would be joining:



We do not want yes-people. I want tea
mbers who think for themselves, who speak up when something does not make sense, and who bring their own ideas to the table. I do not want to be the smartest person in the room. I want to surround myself with people whose input I genuinely value, and I want them to know that their contributions matter. If you see a better way to do something, I expect you to say so.



We value people, not just productivity. I have two daughters. I care for my elderly mother. I understand that life happens and family comes first. What I ask for in return is honesty, reliability, and communication. If something comes up, just tell me. We will figure it out together.



AI is how we work. This is not a traditional VA role. I use Claude (by Anthropic) as my primary AI tool, along with ChatGPT, and I am actively building workflows with Claude Code and Claude Cowork. Every document,
, spreadsheet, and communication goes through AI first. If you are the kind of person who opens an AI tool before you open a blank document, you are going to love working with us. If you are not there yet but are genuinely excited to learn, keep reading.



What This Role Looks Like

The primary need is for an AI-Powered Operations Coordinator who sits at the center of our team and keeps everything moving. Think of it as a step above a traditional virtual assistant. You would be the connective tissue between me, our tea
mbers, and our projects.



That said, we also have needs in marketing support, social media, general administration, and research. If the coordinator role does not feel like the right fit, your application might still lead to a different opportunity. We are hiring for more than one position, and we would rather find the right person for the right seat than miss good people because of a narrow job title.



Day-to-day, this role involves:



• Coordinating tasks and deliverables across multiple tea
mbers in different time zones

• Tracking project status and following up with people to make sure work gets done on schedule

• Creating and maintaining tracking systems (spreadsheets, project boards, dashboards) using AI tools

• Drafting, editing, and formatting professional documents,
s, and messages using AI

• Organizing cloud storage (Google Drive, Dropbox) with clear naming and version control

• Researching tools, vendors, and solutions as needed

• Supporting operations across architectural design, real estate management, and AI agency development

• Occasionally joining phone or video calls with U.S.-based clients and tea
mbers



What Would Really Excite Us

If you can do the day-to-day tasks above, that is great. But what would truly set you apart is the ability to build systems, not just follow them.



We are actively building repeatable workflows and operational systems using tools like Claude Code and Claude Cowork. The person who grows into this role at its highest level is someone who can help us create workflows that the whole team can use, build templates and trackers that scale as we grow, and document processes so clearly that a new tea
mber could follow them without hand-holding.



This is the kind of person we would invest in heavily. If you demonstrate that you can build systems and create replicable processes using AI, we will give you every resource you need to succeed (more on that below).



What You Need to Bring

You do not need to check every box. But you do need to be honest about where you are and genuinely willing to grow.



• AI-First Mindset: You actively use AI tools like ChatGPT, Claude, or similar in your daily work, or you are genuinely eager to make AI your primary working tool. You should be comfortable using AI to draft
s, build spreadsheets, summarize documents, create trackers, and research solutions.

• Strong Written English: Most of our communication is text-based. You need to write clearly and professionally.

• Organizational Ability: You will be tracking multiple projects and people simultaneously. If staying organized under complexity is not natural for you, this role will be overwhelming.

• Reliability and Responsiveness: Respond to messages promptly. Meet deadlines. Communicate proactively if something is delayed. Have your messaging apps on your phone so you can see urgent messages even when you are away from your computer.

• Self-Training Ability: If I introduce a new tool, I expect you to use AI and online resources to learn it on your own. You need to be the kind of person who figures things out.

• Reliable Internet and Power: You need stable, fast internet and reliable electricity. If your area has frequent outages, tell us what backup plan you have.



What We Provide (We Invest in Our Team)

This is important, and it is something most job postings do not include. We believe that if we want you to work at a high level, we need to give you the tools to do it. Here is what you can expect from us:



• Paid AI Tool Subscriptions: If you prove that you are committed and capable, we will provide paid subscriptions to the AI tools you need to do your job. This includes Claude Pro, ChatGPT Plus, and any other tools that are essential to your workflows. You should not be limited to free tiers when your work depends on these platforms. We will invest in the tools if you invest in the effort.

• Software and Platforms: If the role requires specific project management tools, cloud storage, communication platforms, or other software, we will provide access. You will not be expected to pay for the tools you need to do your work.

• Training and Growth: We do not expect you to know everything on day one. What we expect is that you are open and teachable. If you show initiative and follow-through, we will support your learning with courses, resources, and hands-on mentorship. If there is a certification or skill that would make you more effective, we will talk about how to make that happen.

• Performance-Based Raises: If you are making our lives easier and our operations stronger, your compensation will grow. We believe in rewarding results, not just time.

• A Real Team, Not Just a Job: You will not be an invisible contractor running errands. You will be part of a team that communicates, collaborates, and builds together. Your ideas will be heard. Your feedback will be welcomed. We want people who want to stay.



The bottom line: if you have been using free AI tools because you could not afford the paid versions, but you know you could do so much more with the right resources, this might be exactly the opportunity you have been looking for. Show us what you can do, and we will make sure you have what you need.



Bonus Skills (Not Required)

• Experience with Claude (by Anthropic), Claude Code, or Claude Cowork

• Familiarity with voice AI platforms (Synthflow, Retell, Vapi, GoHighLevel)

• Experience building workflows, SOPs, or operational documentation

• Sales, customer service, or client-facing communication experience

• Social media management or marketing support experience

• Comfortable speaking on phone or video calls with people in the United States

• Real estate, construction, or design industry experience

• Experience coordinating remote teams across multiple countries



Schedule and Compensation

Schedule



• Hours: Starting at 20 hours per week with potential to grow to 40 hours based on performance and mutual fit.

• Time Zone: I am in Florida, U.S. Eastern Time. We will work out a hybrid schedule with some overlap during U.S. hours. Flexibility will be discussed, but willingness to overlap is important.

• Duration: Long-term. I am looking for someone to grow with, not a short-term hire. We will start with a short paid test project so we can both evaluate the fit.



Compensation



• Starting Rate: $5 to $8 USD per hour, depending on experience and AI proficiency.

• Growth: Strong performers who are building systems, managing coordination, and working with AI at a high level can expect to move into the $8 to $10+ range as the role expands.

• Payment: Weekly to start (to build trust on both sides), transitioning to biweekly or monthly as we settle into a rhythm.

• Tools Provided: Paid AI subscriptions, software, and platform access as described above. Your tools are on us.





How to Apply

I read every application personally. To help yours stand out and help me understand where you might fit best, please include the following:



1. A brief summary of your work experience. Who you worked for, what you did, and what tools you used. Specific examples are better than general descriptions.

2. Your AI experience. Which AI tools you use (if any) and one specific example of how you used AI to complete a work task. If you have only used free versions, that is completely fine. Just be honest about your experience and what you would do with better tools.

3. Have you ever built a workflow, system, or process that other people followed? Describe it briefly. It does not have to be complicated. Even a simple tracking system or SOP counts.

4. A screenshot of your internet speed test from 
 showing your download and upload speeds.

5. Your available hours per week and your typical working schedule in your local time.

6. Your English fluency level (reading, writing, speaking, listening).

7. What city or area you live in, and whether you experience frequent power or internet outages. If you do, tell me your backup plan.

8. A sentence or two about your personality and work style. How would you describe yourself? If you have ever taken a personality assessment (Myers-Briggs, DISC, StrengthsFinder, etc.), feel free to share your results.



Important: Applications that do not include the speed test screenshot and at least an honest answer about AI experience will not be reviewed. I am not looking for perfection. I am looking for follow-through.



Also: Even if you are not sure this is the right role for you, apply anyway and tell me what you are good at. We have multiple needs, and the right fit might be something we have not even posted yet.





A Final Note

I have been hiring remotely for many years. I know there are a lot of job postings out there, and I know some employers treat people as disposable. That is not how I operate. I invest in people. I value honesty over polish. I care about the person behind the screen.



If you are reliable, teachable, collaborative, and ready to grow with a team that genuinely appreciates what you bring, I would love to hear from you.



Scott

title: AI Content Automation Specialist — US Insurance & Financial Services Agency $7/hour | Long-Term Role | Rate increases
description:
WHO WE ARE



We are a US-based insurance and financial services agency serving physicians, surgeons, nurses, and business owners across New Jersey and New York. We specialize in disability insurance, employee benefits, and retirement planning. We are building a content-driven lead generation machine and need someone to operate it.



THIS IS NOT A MANUAL CONTENT CREATION JOB.



We use AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT, NotebookLM, Gemini, Canva AI) to generate content. Your job is to operate the AI-powered content pipeline: prompt the AI, review and polish the output, schedule it across 7+ platforms, manage 10+ WordPress websites, build lead capture funnels, track SEO performance, and drive leads into our CRM. You are the operator of an automated system — not the person writing every word from scratch.



If you've never used ChatGPT, Claude, Canva AI, or AI content tools — this role is not for you.

If you love working with AI tools and want to manage a system that produces 50-100+ content pieces per week — keep reading.



WHAT YOU WILL DO

Content Automation (40% of your time)



Use Claude/Gemini/ChatGPT to generate blog articles, LinkedIn posts,
 newsletters, and social media content based on approved weekly topic calendars

Edit and polish AI-generated content for quality, accuracy, and brand voice

Batch-create social media graphics in Canva using templates

Multiply one piece of content into 5-7 platform-specific variations (LinkedIn,
, Instagram,
, Nextdoor, Pinterest,
)

Manage and schedule content across all platforms using scheduling tools (Blotato, or native scheduling)

Create short-form video content using Canva or CapCut templates (not manual video editing — template-based)



WordPress & SEO Management (25% of your time)



Set up new WordPress sites on 10+ parked domains (install WordPress, configure theme, install SEO and lead capture plugins, create standard page templates) — this is your Week 1 priority project

Major lead generation website already has a WordPress site — audit it, optimize it, and use it as the template for all other domains. Potentially rebuild the content to make it more lead attractive.



Manage all WordPress websites: publish blog posts, update pages, maintain plugins, monitor uptime

Optimize every blog post for SEO: keywords, meta descriptions, internal links, schema markup

Research long-tail keywords using free tools (Google Keyword Planner, Ubersuggest, AnswerThePublic)



Submit new content to Google Search Console for indexing

Monitor Google Analytics and Search Console for all domains — weekly traffic report

Create and optimize lead capture forms and popups (WPForms, OptinMonster, GHL, AgentCRM or similar)

Build simple landing pages on WordPress for lead magnets and campaigns

NOTE: Our two main business sites are NOT on WordPress — they are on a separate platform and you will not touch them



LinkedIn Lead Generation (20% of your time)



Manage personal LinkedIn profile: publish posts, engage with comments, respond to messages using pre-approved templates

Send 25-40 targeted connection requests per day to physicians, surgeons, nurses, HR directors, practice managers, and CPAs

Personalize connection request messages using AI-generated templates

Engage in LinkedIn groups and comment on relevant industry posts

Build LinkedIn carousels and document posts in Canva

Track connection acceptance rates, engagement rates, and inbound messages weekly



Lead Generation & CRM Integration (15% of your time)



Ensure all website forms are connected to our CRM (Agent CRM / GoHighLevel) via webhook

Monitor incoming leads — verify auto-tagging and auto-sequences are firing correctly

Flag high-priority leads to us mmediately via WhatsApp

Track leads by source: which domain, which platform, which campaign generated each lead

Compile weekly lead generation report: leads by source, cost per lead (for paid channels), conversion status





TOOLS YOU MUST KNOW (or learn quickly)

Required (must have experience):



ChatGPT / Gemini/ NanoBanana  and/or Claude (AI content generation)

Canva (graphic design, social media templates, presentations)

WordPress (full setup from scratch: installing WordPress, configuring themes, installing plugins, content publishing, basic troubleshooting — NOT just posting on an existing site)

Google Analytics and Google Search Console

LinkedIn (business page management + personal profile management)

Buffer, Hootsuite, or similar social media scheduling tool

Google Sheets (reporting, data tracking)



Strong Plus (will train if needed):



GoHighLevel / Agent CRM (our CRM — similar to HubSpot or ActiveCampaign)

RankMath or Yoast (WordPress SEO plugins)

OptinMonster or ConvertBox (lead capture popups)

Blotato or 
 (content multiplication)

CapCut (short-form video from templates)

n8n (workflow automation — if you know this, major bonus)

Shared hosting management (Hostinger, SiteGround, Cloudways — pointing domains, installing SSL)





THE IDEAL CANDIDATE



Has used AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude, Midjourney, or similar) for at least 12 months to create content — not just played with them, actually used them for client work or personal projects

Has set up WordPress sites from scratch — not just posted on existing ones. You should know how to install WordPress on a hosting account, configure a theme, install and configure plugins (SEO, caching, lead capture), and troubleshoot basic issues

Understands basic SEO: what keywords are, how meta descriptions work, why page speed matters, what a sitemap is

Has done LinkedIn outreach or social media management for a business (not just personal use)

Can write clear, professional English — your writing doesn't need to be literary, but it must be grammatically correct and natural-sounding

Is comfortable working independently with minimal supervision — Director reviews content once per week, not every day

Is organized, meets deadlines, and communicates proactively about blockers

Is available to overlap with US Eastern Time for at least 2 hours daily (for WhatsApp / Zoom communication)



Bonus points if you:



Have experience in insurance, financial services, or healthcare marketing

Have set up and managed multiple WordPress sites on a single hosting plan

Know how to run
 or Google ads (even basic)

Have built landing pages or lead funnels before

Have connected website forms to a CRM (Zapier, 
, webhooks)





SCHEDULE & TERMS



Hours: 25 hours/week (may increase to 30-35 during campaign launches)

Schedule: Flexible — minimum 2 hours overlap with US Eastern Time daily (for WhatsApp check-ins). Remaining hours on your schedule.

Rate: $7/hour USD, paid bi-weekly via PayPal

Start: Immediately upon acceptance

Location: Remote — Philippines preferred



Rate Progression:



90-day review: $8/hour if output targets are met

6-month review: $9/hour with expanded scope (paid ads, advanced AI workflows)

12-month target: $10/hour for full ownership of content and lead gen ecosystem



Monthly Bonus:



$100 bonus if 50+ qualified leads generated across all domains from your content/SEO/social efforts

Additional bonuses for specific milestones (Google page 1 rankings, domain lead targets)





HOW TO APPLY

Begin your application with the word "Proof" as the first word of your message.

Do NOT send a generic "I am a hardworking VA" message. I will ignore it.

Instead, send me ALL THREE of the following:



Start your first sentence with word I am

1. A LinkedIn post draft.

Write a LinkedIn post (
words) targeting orthopedic surgeons about why they need own-occupation disability insurance. Use a hook that stops the scroll. Write it as if you are posting from LinkedIn profile (a financial advisor, not a doctor). You may use ChatGPT or Claude or any other AI to help — I expect you to. I want to see how you prompt AI and how you edit the output.

2. A connection request message.

Write a LinkedIn connection request message (under 300 characters) that Director would send to a homecare agency owner in New York. The goal is to get them to accept the connection — not sell them anything in the first message.

3. A brief note about your AI content experience.

In 3-5 sentences, tell me: which AI tools you use regularly, what kind of content you've created with them, and one specific example of a result you achieved (traffic increase, leads generated, engagement boost, etc.). If you don't have AI content experience, this role is not the right fit.



ABOUT THE ROLE — WHY THIS IS DIFFERENT



This is not a "post on social media 3 times a week" job. You will be operating an AI-powered content machine that produces 50-100+ pieces per week across 10+ websites and 7+ social media platforms. You will be building lead generation funnels that directly drive revenue for the business.



You will work directly with the Director. There is no middle management. Your ideas will be heard and implemented quickly. The business is growing fast and there is room for you to grow with it — into paid ads management, advanced AI automation, or content strategy leadership.

If you want to be part of building something from the ground up, apply now.

title: Virtual Assistant (TikTok Shop Video Editor)
description:
Part-Time Virtual Assistant (TikTok Shop Video Editor) – Remote | Flexible Hours

We’re looking for a reliable, self-motivated Virtual Assistant to join our growing TikTok Shop operation. This is a part-time remote role with flexible hours — perfect for someone who takes pride in their work and doesn’t need to be micromanaged.

What You’ll Be Doing

 ? Editing short-form videos optimized for TikTok Shop (hooks, captions, pacing, sound, etc.)

 ? Managing and organizing content inside GeeLark on a regular basis

 ? Following brand guidelines and content schedules consistently

 ? Communicating updates, flagging issues, and meeting deadlines without being chased

You’re a Great Fit If You:

 ? Have a working iPhone and a reliable laptop that can run GeeLark smoothly

 ? Have hands-on experience editing videos for TikTok or short-form content (please share examples or a portfolio)

 ? Understand TikTok trends, hooks, and what makes people stop scrolling

 ? Have a stable, fast internet connection

 ? Are organized, proactive, and responsive during working hours

 ? Can handle feedback professionally and improve quickly

 ? Have basic file management skills (Google Drive, Dropbox, or similar)

Nice to Have (Not Required)

 ? Experience with TikTok Shop specifically

 ? Familiarity with CapCut, DaVinci Resolve, or similar editing tools

 ? Experience working with GeeLark or similar cloud phone platforms

Requirements

 ? Own iPhone (any modern model)

 ? Laptop capable of running GeeLark comfortably (minimum 8GB RAM recommended)

 ? Reliable high-speed internet

 ? Available for consistent part-time hours each week

title: Elite VA - Systems, Automation & Social Media GUN
description:
We are a boutique mortgage brokerage based in Sydney, Australia seeking a high-performing Virtual Assistant (VA) based in the Philippines to support our growing business



This role is designed for a proactive, high-calibre operator who can work autonomously, improve systems, and help drive efficiency across the business



We are specifically looking for a Top 5% VA — someone who is organised, assertive, detail-oriented, and trusted to get things done without constant supervision.



Role Details

Location: Philippines (Remote)

Hours: Approximately 5–10 hours per week (hours may fluctuate depending on workload)

Industry: Australian Mortgage & Finance



Key Responsibilities

• Provide administrative support for mortgage applications

• Manage client follow-ups and document collection

• Maintain and update CRM systems and client records

• Assist with
 management, scheduling, and task tracking

• Support file preparation and loan submission documentation

• Help implement and refine internal processes to improve efficiency

• Assist with social media content creation and scheduling



Essential Skills & Attributes

• Proactive and assertive – able to take initiative without constant supervision

• Trusted to work autonomously and get the job done

• Excellent attention to detail

• Fast learner who can quickly understand new systems

• Efficient, reliable, and trustworthy

• Strong written and verbal English communication skills

• Highly organised and capable of managing multiple tasks



Technical Skills

• MUST be a Google Suite Ninja (Gemini, AI, NanoBanana, Docs, Sheets, Drive, Gmail, Calendar)

• Experience with the Google AI Suite

• Proven social media experience that can be demonstrated

• Experience using Canva and CapCut

• Zapier experience highly desirable (automation and workflow integration)

• Comfortable learning and managing CRM systems and workflows

• Experience with Broker Engine and/or ApplyOnline preferable but not essential



What We Offer

• Competitive salary

• Flexible working hours

• Remote work with a professional Australian finance business

• Opportunity to build a long-term working relationship

• Exposure to the Australian mortgage industry



Application Instructions (Read Carefully)



To ensure applicants have read this advertisement carefully, please begin your application message with the words: 'Detail is my superpower.' Applications that do not include this phrase will not be considered.



Please include the following in your application:

• A short introduction about yourself

• Your experience as a Virtual Assistant

• Examples of social media work you have created (Canva/CapCut etc.)

• Examples of systems, automations, or efficiencies you have implemented

• Your hourly rate and availability



To help us identify high-performing candidates, please also answer the following questions in your application:



1. Describe a process or workflow you improved for a previous client. What was the problem and what system or automation did you implement?



2. Provide a link or examples of social media content you have created using Canva or CapCut and explain your role in creating it

title: Marketing and E-Commerce Assistant
description:
Multimedia Technology is a dynamic and trusted partner in tomorrow’s technology landscape, connecting people and powering the world’s economy. We simplify the technology supply chain through strong, meaningful relationships.



Working with leading global IT and AV brands such as Sony, Panasonic, Samsung, Synology, BenQ, Nvidia, Fujitsu, and HP, you will promote industry-leading products to some of Australia’s best-known businesses.



As a Marketing and ECommerce Assistant, you will play a key role in elevating our brand presence and driving demand across digital channels. With a strong marketing focus, you will support marketing campaigns, promotions, and product launches while optimising online content and marketplace performance. You will collaborate with internal teams to ensure consistent brand messaging and strong commercial outcomes.



Key Responsibilities:



Create print and digital internal marketing materials, including content, creative assets, and sales collateral



Support and execute digital marketing initiatives including
 marketing, SEO optimisation, keyword research, and performance tracking through Google Analytics



Assist in campaign planning, vendor promotions, and engagement initiatives



Optimise product listings with accurate, compelling descriptions, specifications, imagery, and pricing



Assist with Multimedia Technology’s presence across online marketplaces



Drive automation between Multimedia and customers through EDI/Datafeed integration, ensuring live pricing accuracy.





To succeed in this role, you will have:



Hands-on experience managing eCommerce websites, portals, or online marketplaces



Experience supporting digital marketing activities 
, SEO, content, analytics)



Strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret sales and performance data



A creative and strategic mindset with strong customer orientation



The ability to manage multiple projects in a fast-paced environment



Strong stakeholder management and relationship-building skills



Ability to understand, interpret, and adapt to new technology and IT products



Excellent written and verbal communication skills



Please note you must be available to work Mon - Fri 9am -5.30p
lbourne time. Please attach a resume.

title: Ad Creative Operations Manager / Performance Marketing
description:
Compensation: 120,
,000 PHP per month depending on experience, plus performance bonuses

Location: Fully remote

Schedule: Monday to Friday, starting at 2:00 PM PH time

Employment Type: Full-time

Pay Frequency: Monthly



We're hiring a Creative Operations Manager to join a rapidly scaling direct response ecommerce brand in the health and beauty space (supplements, skincare, pain relief). The company is on track to reach $5M this year while expanding into new verticals.



You'll serve as the operational engine behind our creative department. The founder operates as CMO and drives creative strategy, but needs a right hand to build processes, track performance, and manage the team day to day. If you thrive in environments where nothing is documented yet and love building structure from the ground up, this is a great fit.



Responsibilities

Develop and roll out SOPs and KPI frameworks for the full creative org (strategists, editors, media buyers)

Oversee daily operations across two creative teams, holding the team accountable for output quality and timelines

Facilitate team syncs (twice weekly) and daily standups focused on key metrics: CPL, spend, delivery volume, and creative hit rates

Spot and resolve bottlenecks in the creative production pipeline before they slow things down

Track creative output and performance, ensuring the team ships 15-30 ad variations daily

Evaluate resourcing needs across teams and proactively flag when additional hires are needed as the company grows

Manage cross-functional workflows between creative strategists, copywriters, editors, and media buyers

Report to the founder/CMO on team health, roadblocks, and process improvements



Requirements

5-7+ years in a comparable operations or project management role within an agency or ecommerce setting

Solid grasp of performance marketing fundamentals: media buying, CPL, ad budgets, creative testing methodology

Track record of designing SOPs and KPI systems for growing teams

Experience managing teams of 5 or more people

Extremely organized with a systems-oriented mindset; you bring order to ambiguity

Excellent written and verbal English

Available to start work at 2PM Philippine time



Preferred Qualifications

Hands-on experience with TikTok Ads and Meta Ads Manager

Familiarity with cash-on-delivery ecommerce models

Background in scaling creative production for direct response campaigns

Comfort using AI tools for content creation and creative workflows

Experience working in ClickUp or similar PM platforms

/4aGJwX4

title: On-Camera Presenter / Actor (Male) for Calm, Professional Online Course (Remote)
description:
Overview

We are producing a premium instructional video course for adult men navigating serious, high-stakes life decisions. This is not entertainment, hype, coaching, or influencer content.



We are seeking a male on-camera presenter with a neutral, minimal accent who can deliver structured material calmly, clearly, and credibly — similar in tone and pacing to the reference below:



Reference style (tone, pacing, professionalism):





This is a paid project with potential for additional work if the fit is strong.



What You’ll Be Doing

Recording scripted course modules (scripts provided)

Delivering content with calm authority and emotional neutrality

Avoiding hype, dramatization, or motivational speaking

Recording in a professional-quality studio setup

Delivering finished video files (clean audio, stable framing, neutral background)

This role requires the ability to produce the video end-to-end, not just appear on camera.



Requirements (Read Carefully)

*Voice & Communication

Minimal / neutral accent (American, Canadian, UK, or equivalent neutral)

Clear pronunciation and steady pacing

Comfortable delivering serious subject matter

Able to sound grounded, trustworthy, and composed

*Production Capability

Quiet recording environment

High-quality camera (DSLR or equivalent preferred)

Professional lighting

Clean, broadcast-quality audio (lav mic or condenser)

Ability to maintain consistent quality across all modules

*Professional Standards

Able to follow scripts precisely

Reliable turnaround times

Discretion and confidentiality required



NOT a Fit If You…

Have a strong regional or foreign accent

Use AI avatars or synthetic voices

Prefer influencer, sales, or coaching energy

Cannot handle full video production independently

Are uncomfortable with sensitive life topics



Required Audition Script (READ ALOUD ON VIDEO)

All applicants must submit a short video reading the script below.

This is used to assess clarity, accent neutrality, pacing, and tone.

“When everything feels uncertain, the most important thing is not to react — it’s to get clear.

This guide is designed to help you slow things down, understand what’s actually happening, and make decisions that protect your time, your finances, and your relationship with your children.

There is no pressure here, no promises, and no shortcuts.

Just practical information, delivered calmly, so you can move forward with intention instead of fear.”



Guidelines for the audition video:

60–90 seconds total

Neutral background

Natural speaking pace

No background music

No acting, no dramatization



Deliverables

Full course module recordings (details shared after selection)

Clean, edited video files ready for upload

Consistent audio and visual quality



Compensation

Paid per project or per module (discussed after review)

Competitive pay for qualified professionals

Potential for future recordings if collaboration is successful



How to Apply (Required)

All applications must be submitted through this form:





You will be asked to provide:

A short written introduction

A link to your audition video (reading the script above)

Examples of prior on-camera work (if available)

A brief description of your recording setup

Applications submitted outside this form will not be reviewed.



Final Note

This project prioritizes trust, clarity, and composure.

If you can deliver serious content with calm authority and professional production quality, we’d like to hear from you.

title: Operations-Focused Virtual Assistant (Project-Based, Multi-Project)
description:
JOB OVERVIEW

We are building a small but growing portfolio of structured digital projects and are assembling a long-term VA operations pool.

This is not a full-time role and not tied to a single project.

Instead, we work with VAs who:

Follow instructions precisely

Are comfortable moving between different projects

Prefer clear scope over creative freedom

Can deliver clean, correct work with minimal supervision

If selected, you may be invited to short, well-defined tasks across multiple projects over time.



HOW WORK IS STRUCTURED

All projects in this portfolio share the same operating principles:

Clear written instructions

Fixed scope and deliverables

Tight constraints (no guessing, no improvising)

Emphasis on clarity, accuracy, and trust

Fast feedback cycles

No client calls

No selling, promising, or defending

Projects may involve documentation, setup, cleanup, posting, auditing, delivery, or coordination work depending on fit.

You will not be asked to redesign projects, invent strategies, or “make it better” unless explicitly instructed.



THIS ROLE IS A FIT IF YOU:

Prefer executing defined tasks over open-ended brainstorming

Are comfortable asking questions only when blocked

Can flag unclear instructions instead of guessing

Respect boundaries, tone rules, and scope

Are open to working on different projects over time

Value consistency and correctness over speed or creativity



THIS ROLE IS NOT A FIT IF YOU:

Want to specialize in one specific niche or project only

Prefer creative freedom or “adding your own ideas”

Need constant feedback or reassurance

Want to manage strategy, direction, or decision-making

Are looking for guaranteed hours or a fixed long-term role



COMPENSATION & TIME

Hourly, project-based

Rates vary by role and responsibility (typically $4–9 USD/hr)

Short-term tasks with potential for repeat work

No minimum hours guaranteed

Strong performers are added to our internal VA pool and contacted directly for future work.



HOW TO APPLY (IMPORTANT)

Do not reply to this job post.

All applicants must apply through this form:



The form is the first screen.

Incomplete or low-effort submissions will not be reviewed. 



List any specific system competencies in the artifacts section (GHL, Canva, Microsoft Word, 
, etc.)



Please read the form instructions carefully and answer based on the role type you are best suited for.



FINAL NOTE

We are intentionally selective.

If you enjoy structured work, clear expectations, and operating inside well-defined systems, this will be a good fit.

If not, this is probably not the right environment for you — and that’s okay.

Thank you for applying carefully.

title: VA OPENING — MULTIPLE ROLES (CONTENT, AUTOMATION, OUTREACH)
description:
We are building a small portfolio of structured digital projects and are looking to add reliable, detail-oriented VAs to a short-term talent pool.



This is not a full-time role and not a single long-term position.

It is project-based work with clear instructions, fixed scope, and fast turnaround.



We currently have three distinct VA roles open. You may apply to one role only.



ROLE OPTIONS (Choose ONE)



ROLE A — Content & Canva Guide Builder

Focus: Creating clean, practical PDF guides

You will:

Build a 20–30 page PDF guide in Canva from a provided outline

Use plain English, bullet points, and checklists

Follow strict tone rules (busy reader, no jargon, no hype)

Flag unclear areas instead of guessing

You will NOT:

Write marketing copy

Add creativity beyond instructions

Research competitors

Make promises or guarantees

Required experience:

Prior Canva PDF or guide/ebook creation

Comfort following strict outlines and constraints



ROLE B — Funnel & Automation Builder (GoHighLevel)

Focus: Simple, reliable funnel setup

You will:

Clone a basic GoHighLevel funnel

Connect Stripe checkout

Set up a short
 automation (delivery + follow-up)

Test everything end-to-end

You will NOT:

Write copy from scratch

Build complex automations

Optimize or expand scope beyond instructions

Required experience:

Hands-on GoHighLevel experience

Familiarity with Stripe and basic tagging/automation



ROLE C — Local Outreach & Signal Collection

Focus: Posting and responding in local online communities

You will:

Post short, pre-written messages in local groups (e.g.,
, community forums)

Respond politely using provided scripts

Identify and log:

General interest

 signups

“Do you offer services?” messages

Track daily activity in a simple log

You will NOT:

Spam

Argue or defend

Freelance responses

Handle sensitive or escalated issues

Required experience:

Prior community posting, moderation, or outreach work

Strong written English

High judgment and restraint



ROLE D — Automation Expert

Focus: Creating and refining advanced automations

You will: Use 
, Zapier, etc to build elegant automations to accomplish business tasks.



COMPENSATION & TIME

Hourly: $4–6 USD (role-dependent)

Time commitment: Short-term, project-based

Schedule: Flexible, but deadlines matter



HOW TO APPLY (IMPORTANT)

Do not reply to this job post.

Apply using this Airtable form:





When filling out the form:

List only skills relevant to the role you’re applying for

Answer the reflection and idea questions thoughtfully (we read them)



In the Open Response field, clearly state:

“Applying for: Role A – Content & Canva”

OR

“Applying for: Role B – GHL Funnel”

OR

“Applying for: Role C – Local Outreach”

OR

"Applying for: Role D - Automations"



Applications that do not clearly specify a role will not be reviewed.



WHO THIS IS (AND IS NOT) FOR

This is for VAs who:

Follow instructions precisely

Prefer clear scope over vague creativity

Are comfortable asking questions only when blocked

Care about delivering clean, correct work



This is not for:

Generalists who “can do anything”

People who need constant feedback

Applicants who want to redesign or expand the project



Final Note

Strong performers may be invited to future projects this month.

Thank you for applying carefully.

title: Remote Accounts / Admin Executive
description:
*Accounts/Admin Role Job Description*



*** MUST HAVE PRIOR WORKING EXPERIENCE IN A SINGAPORE COMPANY***



*Key Responsibilities*



#### *Accounting Duties*

- Manage accounts payable (AP) and accounts receivable (AR), including processing invoices, payments, and tracking outstanding balances.

- Reconcile financial statements, including bank statements, to ensure accuracy and resolve discrepancies.

- Prepare and issue invoices to customers for products or services rendered.

- Monitor and follow up on overdue payments to ensure timely collections.

- Maintain updated financial records in accounting software and spreadsheets.

- Assist in preparing monthly financial reports, budgets, and cash flow statements.



#### *Administrative Duties*

- Oversee general office administration, including procurement of office supplies and maintaining office equipment.

- Coordinate with external vendors for services such as IT support or maintenance.

- Provide support to management by preparing reports, scheduling meetings, and handling correspondence.



#### *Order Taking & Service Coordination*

- Assist in processing customer orders accurately and efficiently.

- Coordinate service schedules to ensure timely delivery or execution of services.

- Act as a liaison between customers and the operations team to address service-related issues.

- Maintain clear communication with customers regarding order status or changes.



#### *Invoicing Support*

- Prepare, send, and store invoices for all transactions.

- Reconcile customer payments with bank statements to ensure accuracy.

- Track outstanding payments and follow up with clients when necessary.



#### *General Support*

- Provide backup support for other administrative staff during their absence by managing their tasks effectively.

- Respond to internal and external inquiries related to accounting, administrative matters, or customer orders.



### *Skills & Qualifications*

- Proven experience in an Accounts/Admin role or similar position.

- Strong knowledge of bookkeeping procedures and accounting software (Xero).

- Advanced proficiency in Excel (e.g., financial formulas, creating spreadsheets).

- Excellent organizational skills with strong attention to detail.

- Ability to identify numerical errors and resolve discrepancies efficiently.

- Strong communication skills for liaising with clients, vendors, and internal teams.



### *Preferred Qualifications*

- Bachelor’s degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field is advantageous but not mandatory if relevant experience is demonstrated.

- Experience in service coordination or customer order management is a plus.

title: Line Art Graphic Designer / Etsy Product Designer (Part-Time)
description:
About the Role



We're looking for a creative and trend-aware Line Art Graphic Designer to support our Etsy shop by creating compelling, on-brand print-on-demand (POD) product designs. You’ll play a key role in researching current Etsy trends, generating original graphic concepts, and creating graphics to be placed on product mockups.



This is a long-term, part-time role at $80-$100/month with a steady pace of deliverables. If you love line art, staying ahead of trends, creating visually appealing designs, and working independently, we strongly encourage you to apply!



What You’ll Be Doing



Research trending Etsy products and design themes (based on season, niche, keywords)

Create print-on-demand designs that align with the brand’s voice and aesthetic

Use Canva or Photoshop to create high-quality graphic designs for products

Apply your designs to mockups for multiple color variations

Deliver export-ready images for Etsy listings

Occasionally refresh or improve existing listings and design sets



Design Expectations



Approx. 25–30 finished product designs per month

Each design includes multiple color or product mockups

Design assets must be original, well-researched, and conversion-minded



Tools You’ll Use



Canva

Photoshop or Illustrator (optional, but helpful)

Google Drive (for shared folders, templates, and delivery)

Trend research tools (Etsy search, Pinterest, competitor stores, etc.)



Requirements



1-2 years of graphic design experience

Proficiency in Adobe Photoshop and Illustrator

Experience designing for Etsy is a plus

Excellent time-management skills and ability to meet deadlines

Strong sense of visual design, layout, and composition

Familiarity with print-on-demand products and design best practices

Ability to spot design trends and apply them creatively

Organized, reliable, and able to follow brand direction

Can take feedback and revise quickly when needed



Hours & Compensation



Fixed rate: $80 USD/month

Part-time, remote

Estimated workload: ~25–30 design sets per month

Paid monthly

Potential for long-term growth



To Apply

Please send the following:



A short message introducing yourself and your experience with print-on-demand or Etsy design

A portfolio or samples of recent designs (bonus if related to Etsy or POD)

What design tools you prefer to work with

How you stay up to date on trends or what niches you enjoy designing for



Looking forward to seeing your work!

title: Community and Growth Associate
description:
YSS is a Sydney-based creative agency operating three divisions: YSS Agency (creative services for beauty brands), Can We Go Live (an industry talk show), and Hair Clubs — a B2B SaaS loyalty platform for hair salons that’s growing fast. We are a small team that moves quickly and holds a high bar for everything we put out.



The Role

We’re looking for someone who can own the client experience from signup to ongoing retention. You’ll be the person who makes sure every client feels looked after, every deliverable lands properly, and nothing falls through the cracks. This role is equal parts client comms, light creative production, and proactive problem-solving.



What You’ll Do

Client Success & Comms

 • Own ongoing communication with Hair Clubs salon clients — check-ins, follow-ups, and first response on enquiries

 • Monitor client health and flag at-risk accounts before they churn

 • Manage onboarding comms for new salons signing up through paid ads

 • Follow up with demo bookings who haven’t shown up (SMS +
 sequences)

 • QA client-facing deliverables before they go out — making sure creative matches the client’s brand



Creative Support

 • Design simple client assets in Canva — EDMs, social tiles, QR codes, launch carousels

 • Adapt templates to match each salon’s brand (you’ll be working from established references, not designing from scratch)

 • Support social media posting for Hair Clubs and client accounts as needed

 • Ideating with the creative team on social media posts

 • Scheduling social media

 • Weekly EDM creation for Hair Clubs with news 

 • Broadcast channel management 



What We’re Looking For

 • Fast, warm, and proactive communicator — you reply to clients within 20 minutes, not 3 hours

 • Comfortable in Canva with a good eye for brand consistency

 • Detail-oriented — you check the brief, check the client’s Instagram, check the website before you start

 • Experience in client-facing roles — customer success, account management, community management, or similar

 • Comfortable working across Slack, Notion, and

 • Available during Australian business hours (AEST)

 • Bonus: experience with Shopify, Mailchimp/Klaviyo, or Meta Business Suite



What You Won’t Be Doing

 • Complex graphic design — we have an Art Director for that

 • Running paid ads — that’s handled by the founder

 • Technical development or coding

 • Strategy — you’ll execute on established processes and flag when something’s not working



How To Apply
